Ayahs

حمٓ (۱)
Ha, Meem, (1)
عٓسٓقٓ (۲)
‘Ayn, Seen, Qaf. (2)
كَذَٰلِكَ يُوحِىٓ إِلَيْكَ وَإِلَى ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِكَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۳)
Allah, the All-mighty and the All-wise, thus reveals to you and to those who were before you: (3)
لَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَلِىُّ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (٤)
whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth belongs to Him, and He is the All-exalted, the All-supreme. (4)

Key Points

In the Noble Qur'an, the word "قبلك" (before you), addressed to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, is frequently used, meaning that prophets came before you. However, the word "بعدك" (after you) is never mentioned anywhere, and this indicates the finality (Khātamiyyah) of the Prophet of Islam. Although the Prophet of Islam is the Seal of the Prophets, His name is mentioned before others (in the sequence of the initial letters of this Surah), and this shows His high greatness. The Divine Glory (Izzah), Power, Wisdom, and Greatness are all manifested in the Divine Revelation; therefore, the Revelation and the Word of Allah should not be taken lightly. The noble Prophets are connected to the source of Divine Glory, Power, and Greatness, so whoever opposes them will be destroyed. One of the proofs of the Qur'an's miraculous nature (I'jāz) is that it has constructed sentences and compositions from common and ordinary letters and words, which no one else can produce a similar one to.

Messages

1- When you want to introduce a topic, create sensitivity and attraction for your listeners. «حم. عسق»

2- The method and content of revelation to the Prophets are similar to one another. «كذلك»

3- Revelation is one of the Divine traditions (Sunan). «كذلك يوحى...»

4- All Prophets are connected to one source and receive revelation from one origin. «كذلك يوحى»

5- Revelation originates from the source of Glory (Izzah); do not oppose it, lest you be destroyed. «يوحى... العزیز»

6- Revelation is from the Glorious (Al-Azīz) and Wise (Al-Hakīm) God, and obedience to it grants you glory and steadfastness. «العزیز الحكیم»

7- The descent of revelation is based on wisdom; therefore, it is not revealed to just anyone. «يوحى... الحكيم»

8- Revelation originates from the source of wisdom, and thus its contents are enduring and immune to flaw. «يوحى... الحكيم»

9- The Glory of Allah is combined with His wisdom and competence. «العزیز الحكیم»

10- The system of legislation (Tashrī', i.e., revelation and guidance) is the right of the One who controls the system of creation (Takwīn, i.e., the heavens and the earth). «له ما فى السموات و...»

11- Revelation is for our growth; otherwise, He is completely self-sufficient. «له ما فى السموات... العلى العظیم»

12- True ownership belongs to Allah; others merely imagine ownership. «له ما فى السموات...»

13- Allah's absolute ownership over the universe is the support for His Glory and Wisdom. «العزیز الحكیم له ما فى السموات...»

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Ash-Shura

This Surah has fifty-three verses and was revealed in Mecca. Its name is "Shura" (Consultation) due to verse 38, which refers to the issue of consultation. This Surah addresses topics related to the fundamentals of religion (Usūl ad-Dīn), such as Monotheism (Tawhīd), the Resurrection (Ma'ād), and Prophethood (Nubuwwah), as well as ethical and social issues. Among the 29 Surahs that begin with the disconnected letters (Hurūf Muqaṭṭa'ah), the longest sequence of disconnected letters appears in this Surah. Interestingly, this Surah has solved the problem of the meaning of the disconnected letters, because immediately following these letters, it states: «كذلك يوحى» (Thus He reveals), meaning that the Revelation is composed of these very letters of the Arabic alphabet, which are available to all of you; if you can, then produce something similar to it.

Ayahs

تَكَادُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتُ يَتَفَطَّرْنَ مِن فَوْقِهِنَّ ۚ وَٱلْمَلَـٰٓئِكَةُ يُسَبِّحُونَ بِحَمْدِ رَبِّهِمْ وَيَسْتَغْفِرُونَ لِمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ هُوَ ٱلْغَفُورُ ٱلرَّحِيمُ (٥)
The heavens are about to be rent apart from above them, while the angels celebrate the praise of their Lord and plead for forgiveness for those [faithful] who are on the earth. Indeed, Allah is the All-forgiving, the All-merciful! (5)
وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ ٱللَّهُ حَفِيظٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَمَآ أَنتَ عَلَيْهِم بِوَكِيلٍ (٦)
As for those who have taken awliya besides Him, Allah is watchful over them, and you are not their keeper. (6)

Key Points

What is Revelation (Wahy) that due to its grandeur, the heavens are nearly torn apart—heavens which are layered, «سبع سموات طباقا»(1), which are firm, «سبعاً شدادا»(2), and which are exemplary in their firmness? «ءانتم اشدّ خلقاً ام السماء بناها»(3) Heavens that contain thousands of galaxies, and every galaxy contains solar systems, and every solar system contains planets, some of which are millions of times larger than the Earth, and human intellect and science have not yet encompassed even the closest of them. This is the grandeur of Revelation. In the blessed Surah Maryam, it is stated: «تكاد السموات یتفطّرن منه و تنشق الارض و تخرّ الجبال هدّا أن دعوا للرّحمن ولدا»(4). The polytheists imagined a son for Allah; due to this corrupt belief, the heavens are nearly shattered. In addition to being responsible for managing the order of existence, the angels also pray for human beings—angels who never disobey «لا یعصون اللّه ما أمرهم»(5)—they pray and seek forgiveness (Istighfar) for sinful individuals. Yes, the pure must also think of others. Of course, the angels' seeking of forgiveness is a reflection of Divine grace upon the servants, because they do nothing except by Allah's command. Based on this Ayah, the angels pray for all those on Earth. «یستغفرون لمن فى الارض». Based on Ayah 7 of the blessed Surah Ghafir, the angels pray for the believers. «و یستغفرون للّذین آمنوا». And it is interesting that this seeking of forgiveness is effective only on the condition that the believers themselves also seek forgiveness. «فاغفر للّذین تابوا و اتبعوا سبیلك». Let us not doubt Allah's pardon. In the phrase «ألا انّ اللّه هو الغفور الرحیم», several types of emphasis are present. («ألا», «انّ», «هو», the nominal sentence structure, the form Ghafoor instead of Ghafir, and the definite article Alif and Lam in «الغفور»). Allah has completed the argument (Itmam al-Hujjah) with the people, making Revelation His tradition (Sunnah) and the Prophet the intermediary of Revelation, yet people still turn to others. «من دونه اولیاء اللّه». In the Quran and narrations, praise (Hamd) is usually mentioned alongside glorification (Tasbih). «یسبحون بحمد ربّهم», «سبحان ربى الاعلى و بحمده».

1) Al-Mulk, 3. 2) An-Naba, 12. 3) An-Nazi'at, 27. 4) Maryam, 90 - 91. 5) At-Tahrim, 6.

Messages

1- The heavens are affected by Revelation; how is it that some human beings are not affected? «یوحى... تكاد السموات یتفطرن»

2- One of the etiquettes of supplication (Du'a) and seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) is to first offer praise to Allah. «یسبحون... یستغفرون»

3- The prayer of the angels is answered. «یستغفرون... ان اللّه هو الغفور الرحیم» (Yes, praying on behalf of others has an effect.)

4- No responsibility or task (even managing the affairs of existence) should prevent supplication and seeking forgiveness. (The angels who are responsible for managing the affairs of existence are constantly engaged in glorifying Allah and praying for human beings). «یسبحون بحمد ربّهم... یستغفرون»

5- Seeking forgiveness leads to two definite graces: forgiveness (Maghfirah) and mercy (Rahmah). «یستغفرون... الغفور الرحیم»

6- Since Revelation descends from above and the heavens are its path «سبع طرائق»(6), every heaven it passes through is nearly torn apart. «یتفطرن من فوقهن»

7- Polytheism (Shirk), in all its dimensions, is condemned. «اتّخذوا من دونه اولیاء»

8- The polytheists should know that Allah preserves their beliefs and deeds for punishment. «اللّه حفیظ علیهم»

9- The people's rejection should not cause discouragement; the Prophet must fulfill his duty. «و ما انت علیهم بوكیل»

10- The Prophet's duty is the conveyance of Revelation; if people turn to others, the Prophet has no further responsibility. «ما انت علیهم بوكیل»

11- The Prophet had great eagerness and commitment toward guiding the people. «ما انت علیهم بوكیل»

12- The Prophet is responsible for guiding the people, not for compelling them to accept. «ما انت علیهم بوكیل»

6) Al-Mu'minun, 17.

Ayah

وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ قُرْءَانًا عَرَبِيًّا لِّتُنذِرَ أُمَّ ٱلْقُرَىٰ وَمَنْ حَوْلَهَا وَتُنذِرَ يَوْمَ ٱلْجَمْعِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ ۚ فَرِيقٌ فِى ٱلْجَنَّةِ وَفَرِيقٌ فِى ٱلسَّعِيرِ (۷)
Thus have We revealed to you an Arabic Quran that you may warn [the people of] the Mother of the Towns and those around it, and warn [them] of the Day of Gathering, in which there is no doubt, [whereupon] a part [of mankind] will be in paradise and a part will be in the Blaze. (7)

Key Points

Based on narrations, the intent of Umm al-Qura (Mother of Cities) is Mecca. In some narrations, we read: The first point that emerged from the water was Mecca, hence it has been called the "Mother of Settlements."

One of the names of the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) is Yawm al-Jam' (The Day of Gathering), the day when all people will be gathered in one place, the day when the body will be gathered with the soul, the deed with the person, the oppressor with the oppressed, and the punishment and reward with its doer.

In the previous Ayah, He addressed the Prophet, saying: You are not the people's representative (wakil); in this Ayah, He says: Your duty is to warn and admonish (indhar).

Question: Although the Noble Qur'an has repeatedly narrated the doubt and astonishment of the disbelievers regarding the Resurrection (Ma'ad), why does He then say: There is no doubt in it. «لا ریب فیه»?

Answer: The phrase «لا ریب فیه» (there is no doubt in it) appears eleven times in the Qur'an regarding the Resurrection, four times regarding the Qur'an, and once regarding the appointed term (ajal). This means that by observing the examples of resurrection in nature, the appearance of spring and autumn, and your own creation, it is not appropriate to doubt the possibility of the dead being brought back to life. It does not mean that no one doubts, but rather that there should be no doubt.

Messages

1- Just as previous prophets spoke the language of their people, the Qur'an was also revealed in the Arabic language. «و كذلك اوحینا... قرآناً عربیّاً»

2- The words (phrasing) of the Qur'an were revealed to the Prophet exactly as they are. «اوحینا الیك قرآناً عربیّاً»

3- The Qur'an is in the Arabic language, and its translation into any other language does not carry the ruling (hukm) of the Qur'an. «قرآناً عربیّاً» (Because human understanding cannot fully grasp the realities of the Qur'anic phrases and words, and translation is based on the translator's personal understanding.)

4- The main goal of divine revelations is their practical outcome. «اوحینا... لتنذر»

5- Whoever warns others must possess the necessary awareness and insight himself. «اوحینا... لتنذر»

6- In the method of propagation, we must first recognize the audience and their needs. (The primary need of the ignorant society was warning/admonition). «لتنذر»

7- In propagation, attention must be paid to regional, spatial, and strategic priorities. (Give priority to central and pivotal points.) «امّ القُرى و من حولها»

8- Propagation must begin with oneself and one's immediate surroundings. «لتنذر امّ القرى»

9- The call (da'wah) of the Prophet of Islam was gradual and had multiple stages. «لتنذر امّ القرى و من حولها»

10- Among the subjects of warning, the issue of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) plays a special role. «و تنذر یوم الجمع»

11- The eloquence (fasahah) of the Qur'an plays an important role in warning people. «عربیّاً لتنذر» (Based on the interpretation that the word «عربى» refers to eloquence.)

12- Although the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) is the Day of Gathering, it is also the Day of Separation and Distinction. «فریق فى الجنه و فریق فى السعیر»

Ayahs

وَلَوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ لَجَعَلَهُمْ أُمَّةً وَٰحِدَةً وَلَـٰكِن يُدْخِلُ مَن يَشَآءُ فِى رَحْمَتِهِۦ ۚ وَٱلظَّـٰلِمُونَ مَا لَهُم مِّن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ (۸)
Had Allah wished, He would have surely made them one community; but He admits whomever He wishes into His mercy, and the wrongdoers do not have any guardian or helper. (8)
أَمِ ٱتَّخَذُوا۟ مِن دُونِهِۦٓ أَوْلِيَآءَ ۖ فَٱللَّهُ هُوَ ٱلْوَلِىُّ وَهُوَ يُحْىِ ٱلْمَوْتَىٰ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍ قَدِيرٌ (۹)
Have they taken awliya besides Him? [Say,] ‘It is Allah, who is the [true] Wali, and He revives the dead, and He has power over all things. (9)

Key Points

Allah is Wise, and all His actions are based on wisdom, even if human thought cannot grasp the wisdom behind His deeds. Therefore, in instances where the Quran states: that Allah grants honor or humiliation to whomever He wills, the meaning is this: those who, through faith and righteous deeds, acquire the merit to receive honor and guidance, Allah places them on the path of His mercy and guidance. However, those who, through disbelief (kufr), hypocrisy (nifaq), and obstinacy, blind themselves to the potential for felicity, Allah does nothing for them. If you enter a house and see a thief inside, you immediately lock the door on him until you call the police. In this scenario, although you have closed the door on him, the thief himself provided the means for the door to be closed. Similarly, Allah seals hearts, misguides or humiliates certain groups, but this Divine wrath is due to their own corrupt beliefs and actions. The word wali (ولىّ) is singular, and the word awliya (اولیاء) is plural, because monotheists accept only the guardianship (wilayah) of Allah, whereas those who separate themselves from Allah have a different guardian every moment.

Messages

  1. The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is to leave humans free to choose their own path; otherwise, He could have compelled everyone onto the path of truth. «ولو شاء اللّه»
  2. Those who deviate from the path of truth are, in reality, wronging themselves. «والظالمون»
  3. The wrongdoer is deprived of all types of help on the Day of Judgment. «ما لهم من ولى ولا نصیر» — neither help from a superior authority «من ولى» nor help from subordinates or friends. «ولا نصیر»
  4. Respond to deviant views and ideas. «ام اتّخذوا - فاللّه هوالولىّ...»
  5. Accepting [protectors] other than Allah is subject to blame and reproach. «ام اتّخذوا»
  6. Claims must be accompanied by proof. «فاللّه هوالولىّ و هو یحى الموتى و هو على كلّ شى ء قدیر»
  7. Any form of guardianship (wilayah) or supervision that is not in line with Divine guardianship is illegitimate and void. «فاللّه هو الولىّ»
  8. The guardianship (wilayah) of Allah is absolute, extensive, and eternal. «هوالولىّ»
  9. Guardianship (wilayah) is the right of the One who possesses power. «هو الولىّ... و هو على كلّ شى ء قدیر»

Ayah

وَمَا ٱخْتَلَفْتُمْ فِيهِ مِن شَىْءٍ فَحُكْمُهُۥٓ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبِّى عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ وَإِلَيْهِ أُنِيبُ (۱٠)
Whatever thing you may differ about, its judgement is with Allah. That is Allah, my Lord. In Him, I have put my trust, and to Him do I turn penitently. (10)

Key Points

As long as humanity exists, differences of opinion will also exist, and as long as differences of opinion exist, recourse to divine law and religion is necessary. Therefore, one cannot claim that there is no need for religion. In this Ayah, the word tawakkaltu (توكّلت - I have relied) is mentioned in the past tense (māḍī), and the word unīb (اُنیب - I turn/repent) is mentioned in the present/future tense (muḍāri‘). Perhaps this suggests that tawakkul (reliance) arises from a firm faith, but inābah (turning back/repentance) is necessary every day and every moment. The words ‘alayhi (علیه - upon Him) and ilayhi (الیه - to Him) precede tawakkaltu and unīb. This means: do not rely upon anyone else, nor turn to others for recourse.

Messages

1- Religion is not merely the answer to moral and theological issues; rather, religion provides the answer to whatever you differ upon regarding political, economic, and other issues. «و ما اختلفتم من شى ء»

2- There is no deadlock in Islam. «و ما اختلفتم... فحكمه الى اللّه»

3- Resolving disputes is among the prerogatives of Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «ذلكم اللّه ربى»

4- When disputes arise, refer back to Allah, rely upon Him, and turn to Him (in repentance/submission), and do not worry about implementing whatever ruling He issues. «فحكمه الى اللّه... علیه توكّلت و الیه انیب»

5- Reliance (Tawakkul) and turning back (Inābah) are the results of faith in Allah’s Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «اللّه ربّى علیه توكّلت و الیه انیب»

Ayah

فَاطِرُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ جَعَلَ لَكُم مِّنْ أَنفُسِكُمْ أَزْوَٰجًا وَمِنَ ٱلْأَنْعَـٰمِ أَزْوَٰجًا ۖ يَذْرَؤُكُمْ فِيهِ ۚ لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِۦ شَىْءٌ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْبَصِيرُ (۱۱)
The originator of the heavens and the earth, He made for you mates from your own selves, and mates of the cattle, by which means He multiplies you. Nothing is like Him, and He is the All-hearing, the All-seeing. (11)

Key Points

The word "Fāṭir" (فاطر) means the creation of something that had no prior likeness. Of course, "faṭara" (فَطَر) literally means to cleave or separate, and this expression might refer to the fact that the earth and the heavens were initially a single, dense mass which was cleaved due to an explosion, and the spheres were separated. And Allah knows best (واللّه العالم). Allah cannot be likened or compared to anything. He is "al-Ḥayy" (the Ever-Living), but His life is separate from the life of all living beings. In clause 71 of the Du'a Jawshan Kabir, we read: «یا حیّاً قبل كلّ حىّ، یا حیّاً بعد كلّ حىّ، یا حىّ الّذى لیس كمثله حىّ، یا حىّ الّذى لا یشاكله حىّ، یا حىّ الّذى لا یحتاج الى حىّ، یا حىّ الّذى یمیت كلّ حىّ، یا حیّاً لم یرث الحیاة من حىّ، یا حىّ الّذى یرزق كلّ حىّ، یا حىّ الّذى یحیى الموتى، یا حىّ و یا قیّوم لا تأخذه سنة ولا نوم» If we only pay attention to the attribute of Allah's life, we see that Allah is before every living being and after every living being; He is a Living Being who has no partner in life, is not in need of any other living being, and causes all living beings to die.

Messages

1- The creation of the heavens and the earth is completely original and innovative. «فاطر»

2- The purpose of pairing (zaujiyyah) is the continuation of the lineage. («یذرؤكم» means multiplication, which follows the word «ازواج» [pairs]).

3- Allah is exalted above having a spouse. (Pairing exists in humans and animals, but Allah has no spouse.) «لیس كمثله شى ء»

4- Although nothing is like Allah, He is connected to everything; He is the Hearer and the Seer, and He observes all creation. «لیس كمثله شى ء و هو السمیع البصیر»

5- Allah's seeing and hearing are different from all other forms of seeing and hearing. «لیس كمثله شى ء و هو السمیع البصیر»

Ayah

لَهُۥ مَقَالِيدُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ يَبْسُطُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِمَن يَشَآءُ وَيَقْدِرُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ بِكُلِّ شَىْءٍ عَلِيمٌ (۱۲)
To Him belong the keys of the heavens and the earth: He expands the provision for whomever He wishes, and tightens it [for whomever He wishes]. Indeed He has knowledge of all things.’ (12)

Key Points

“Maqālīd” is the plural of “Miqlīd,” meaning key. A key is a means of locking and unlocking, and the One who holds the keys to the heavens and the earth can open or close provision (Rizq). The keys to existence are sometimes material factors that mediate grace (fayḍ), such as rain, which is the means by which the soil and earth are brought to life, “فاحیى به الارض” (7). At other times, they are Divine Names, an example of which we read in the Du'a al-Simāt: “اسئلك باسمك الّذى اذا دُعیت به على مغالق ابواب السماء للفتح بالرّحمة انفتحت...” O God! I call upon You by that Name which, if invoked upon the locked doors of the heavens, they are opened by Your mercy.

The expansion or restriction of provision is not a sign of Divine favor or wrath, because the Quran states: “فلا تعجبك اموالهم ولا اولادهم انّما یرید اللّه لیعذّبهم بها” (8). Do not let the wealth and children of the disbelievers astonish you, for Allah has willed to punish them with these very means in this world.

Although the expansion or restriction of provision and livelihood is in the hands of Allah, man should not cease striving. The Quran commands: “وابتغوا من فضل اللّه” (9). Go forth seeking the bounty of Allah and strive to acquire wealth.

Provision (Rizq) is not always material, for we read in supplication: “الّلهم ارزقنى توفیق الطاعة و بُعد المعصیة” O God! Grant me the provision of success in obeying You and distance from sin.

The Quran has taught us the keys to expansion, stating: “لئن شكرتم لازیدنّكم” (10). If you are grateful, He will increase your provision. “استغفروا ربّكم... یرسل السماء علیكم مدرارا” (11). Seek forgiveness from your Lord; He will send down abundant rain upon you. “و مَن یتّق اللّه یجعل له مَخرجاً و یرزقه من حیث لا یحتسب” (12). Whoever is mindful of Allah, He will make a way out for him and provide for him from where he does not expect.

7) بقره، 164. 8) توبه، 55 . 9) جمعه، 10. 10) ابراهیم، 7. 11) هود، 52 . 12) طلاق، 2 - 3.

Messages

1- The effective factors in existence are solely in the hands of Allah. “له مقالید”

2- The expansion and restriction of provision are not accidental; they are in the hands of the Wise God. “یبسط الرزق... و یقدر”

3- The expansion of provision does not depend on cleverness; it is in the hands of Allah. “لمن یشاء”

4- The distribution of provision by Allah is based on knowledge. “انّه بكلّ شى ء علیم”

Ayah

۞ شَرَعَ لَكُم مِّنَ ٱلدِّينِ مَا وَصَّىٰ بِهِۦ نُوحًا وَٱلَّذِىٓ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ وَمَا وَصَّيْنَا بِهِۦٓ إِبْرَٰهِيمَ وَمُوسَىٰ وَعِيسَىٰٓ ۖ أَنْ أَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلدِّينَ وَلَا تَتَفَرَّقُوا۟ فِيهِ ۚ كَبُرَ عَلَى ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ مَا تَدْعُوهُمْ إِلَيْهِ ۚ ٱللَّهُ يَجْتَبِىٓ إِلَيْهِ مَن يَشَآءُ وَيَهْدِىٓ إِلَيْهِ مَن يُنِيبُ (۱۳)
He has prescribed for you the religion which He had enjoined upon Noah and which We have [also] revealed to you, and which We had enjoined upon Abraham, Moses and Jesus, declaring, ‘Maintain the religion, and do not be divided in it.’ Hard on the polytheists is that to which you summon them. Allah chooses for it whomever He wishes, and He guides to it whomever returns penitently [to Him]. (13)

Key Points

In this Ayah, the names of all five Prophets of Resolve (Ulul-'Azm) are mentioned: Noah, Abraham, Moses, Jesus (علیهم السلام), and the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله). The core message of the Prophets was one, and all those great figures invited people to Monotheism (Tawhid) and the Resurrection (Ma'ad), piety (Taqwa) and justice, prayer (Salat) and fasting (Sawm), kindness to parents, and caring for the deprived. Shar'a (شَرَع) comes from Shari'ah (شریعة), meaning a path that leads people to the bank of large rivers, such as the Shari'ah of Alqamah for reaching the water of the Euphrates River. Since the rulings of religion are the path to reaching perfection, they are called Shari'ah. In the Quran, we do not have the word "Adyan" (religions, plural), because the Divine religion is but one.

Messages

1- Allah established the divine laws (Shari'ahs) for people based on His infinite knowledge. «انّه بكلّ شى ء علیم شرع لكم...»

2- Religion and Shari'ah are in line with human interests. «شرع لكم»

3- Noah is the first Prophet who possessed a comprehensive divine law (Shari'ah). «شرع لكم من الدین ما وصّى به نوحا»

4- Islam is the comprehensive religion and encompasses the teachings of all previous Prophets. «ما وصّى به نوحا... و ما وصّینا به ابراهیم و موسى و عیسى»

5- True unity is possible under the shade of the Divine religion. «اقیموا الدین ولا تتفرقوا»

6- Religious differences are the obstacle to establishing religion and implementing its rulings. «اقیموا الدین و لاتتفرقوا»

7- The unity of the believers is difficult for the polytheists (Mushrikin). «لا تتفرّقوا... كبر على المشركین»

8- Invite the polytheists to the truth, even if it is burdensome for them. «كبر على المشركین ما تدعوهم الیه»

9- Prophethood is a Divine office, and Allah chooses the individuals He deems worthy. «یجتبى الیه»

10- Allah chooses the Prophets for His servitude before they become leaders of the people. «اللّه یجتبى الیه »

11- Man creates the grounds for receiving Divine favors through his own actions. «یهدى الیه من ینیب»

Ayah

وَمَا تَفَرَّقُوٓا۟ إِلَّا مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْعِلْمُ بَغْيًۢا بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ إِلَىٰٓ أَجَلٍ مُّسَمًّى لَّقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ أُورِثُوا۟ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ لَفِى شَكٍّ مِّنْهُ مُرِيبٍ (۱٤)
They did not divide [into sects] except after the knowledge had come to them, out of envy among themselves; and were it not for a prior decree of your Lord [granting them reprieve] until a specified time, decision would have been made between them. Indeed those who were made heirs to the Book after them are in grave doubt concerning it. (14)

Key Points

In this Ayah, "جاءهم العلم" (knowledge came to them) is mentioned once, and "اورثوا الكتاب" (they inherited the Book) is mentioned once. This means that despite knowing the truth and being the inheritors of the Divine Book, they still follow the path of division. Doubt (or skepticism) is a good passage (or pathway) but not a good stopping point. The doubt that arises naturally and serves as a prelude to research and investigation is valuable, but if doubt stems from obstinacy and suspicion and causes stagnation and indifference, it is counter-value.

Messages

1- Despite the prophets' recommendation for unity, people still differed. "لاتتفرّقوا... و ما تفرّقوا..."

2- Most of the divisions that have appeared in religion originate from the scholars and the learned. "تفرّقوا... من بعد ما جائهم العلم"

3- The source of most divisions is envy, oppression, and excessive ambition (or seeking superiority). "و ما تفرّقوا الا... بغیاً بینهم"

4- The reason for the divine respites is His Lordship, so that everyone may show their true essence under the shade of freedom and opportunity. "سبقت من ربّك"

5- Granting respite is one of the divine traditions (Sunan). "الى اجلٍ مسمّى"

6- The divine respites last until a specified time. "الى اجلٍ مسمّى"

7- Doubt that is accompanied by suspicion is destructive doubt. "لفى شك منه مریب"

Ayah

فَلِذَٰلِكَ فَٱدْعُ ۖ وَٱسْتَقِمْ كَمَآ أُمِرْتَ ۖ وَلَا تَتَّبِعْ أَهْوَآءَهُمْ ۖ وَقُلْ ءَامَنتُ بِمَآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ مِن كِتَـٰبٍ ۖ وَأُمِرْتُ لِأَعْدِلَ بَيْنَكُمُ ۖ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّنَا وَرَبُّكُمْ ۖ لَنَآ أَعْمَـٰلُنَا وَلَكُمْ أَعْمَـٰلُكُمْ ۖ لَا حُجَّةَ بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكُمُ ۖ ٱللَّهُ يَجْمَعُ بَيْنَنَا ۖ وَإِلَيْهِ ٱلْمَصِيرُ (۱٥)
So summon to this [unity of religion], and be steadfast, just as you have been commanded, and do not follow their desires, and say, ‘I believe in whatever Book Allah has sent down. I have been commanded to do justice among you. Allah is our Lord and your Lord. Our deeds belong to us and your deeds belong to you. There is no quarrel between us and you. Allah will bring us together and toward Him is the destination.’ (15)

Key Points

Practicing justice is the result of a series of beliefs and convictions: recognizing the path of truth and the injunctions of the Divine Prophets, setting aside desires, believing in being present in the Divine Court, and receiving a reward commensurate with the deed. In this Ayah, the command for justice is mentioned alongside all these beliefs.

Messages

1- Against divisions arising from excessive demands, one must be steadfast in the path of religion. «فلذلك فادع واستقم»

2- Propagating religion is accompanied by difficulties that must be endured. «فادع واستقم»

3- Steadfastness is valuable only when it is based on truth and duty; otherwise, it yields nothing but obstinacy. «و استقم كما اُمرت»

4- What opposes the command of Allah is following the desires of the people. «كما اُمرت و لا تتّبع اهوائهم»

5- Following the desires of the people is the affliction of inviting others to the path of truth. «ولا تتبع اهوائهم»

6- The Divine leader must adhere to the principles of his call and not surrender to the illegitimate demands of the people. «قل آمنت بما...»

7- Islam accepts all heavenly books. «امنت بما انزل اللّه الیك من كتاب»

8- The demand for justice is the desire of all Divine religions. «لاعدل بینكم»

9- Prophets must be rulers and influential so that they can implement justice in society. «لاعدل بینكم»

10- Stating commonalities is the best ground for invitation (Da'wah). «اللّه ربّنا و ربّكم»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يُحَآجُّونَ فِى ٱللَّهِ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا ٱسْتُجِيبَ لَهُۥ حُجَّتُهُمْ دَاحِضَةٌ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ وَعَلَيْهِمْ غَضَبٌ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ (۱٦)
Those who argue concerning Allah, after His call has been answered, their argument stands refuted with their Lord, and upon them shall be [His] wrath, and there is a severe punishment for them. (16)

Key Points

Muḥājjah (contention) comes from Ḥajj, meaning intention (or purpose), and it refers to a type of dialogue where the speaker's intention is to prove or disprove something. When a clear path has been established for you, do not abandon it due to doubt or temptation. When you have attained faith in someone, do not abandon him based on rumor. When a good and logical custom, tradition, or practice has been established in society, do not subject it to controversy.

Messages

1- After you have recognized Allah through reason and innate nature (fitra), do not dispute. «یحاجّون فى اللّه من بعد ما استجیب له»

2- Polytheism (shirk) lacks validity, and polytheists have no logic. «حجّتهم داحضة»

3- Attempts to cast doubt upon the believers result in divine wrath and punishment. «یحاجّون... علیهم غضب و لهم عذاب»

Ayah

ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ بِٱلْحَقِّ وَٱلْمِيزَانَ ۗ وَمَا يُدْرِيكَ لَعَلَّ ٱلسَّاعَةَ قَرِيبٌ (۱۷)
It is Allah who has sent down the Book with the truth and [He has sent down] the Balance. What do you know—maybe the Hour is near! (17)

Key Points

One of the names of the Day of Judgment (Qiyamah) is "الساعة" (The Hour), because it arrives suddenly and unexpectedly.

Messages

1- Falsehood has no path in the Noble Qur’an. «انزل الكتاب بالحقّ»

2- The Qur’an possesses a very sublime truth, which has been revealed for our felicity. «انزل الكتاب»

3- The Qur’an is the scale (Mizan) and the means of distinguishing truth from falsehood. «الكتاب بالحق و المیزان»

4- The Noble Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) is also unaware of the time and hour of the Day of Judgment. «و ما یدریك»

5- Do not become entangled in illusory and prolonged hopes that cause the delay of repentance and righteous deeds, for perhaps the Hour is near. «لعل الساعة قریب»

Ayah

يَسْتَعْجِلُ بِهَا ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهَا ۖ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مُشْفِقُونَ مِنْهَا وَيَعْلَمُونَ أَنَّهَا ٱلْحَقُّ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يُمَارُونَ فِى ٱلسَّاعَةِ لَفِى ضَلَـٰلٍۭ بَعِيدٍ (۱۸)
Those who do not believe in it ask [you] to hasten it, but those who have faith are apprehensive of it, and know that it is true. Indeed those who are in doubt about the Hour are in extreme error! (18)

Key Points

If lost individuals are nearby, there is hope for finding and saving them, but if they are far away, finding them is difficult or impossible. «لفى ضلال بعید» (Indeed, in a far-off error.) The disbelievers and polytheists were always hasty in response to the warnings of the prophets regarding the Day of Judgment, saying: «متى هذا الوعد» (When is this promise?) When is the time for these threats? Why are they not fulfilled? Although haste is criticized in many matters, it is recommended in cases such as establishing prayer, burying the deceased, marrying in youth, paying debts, repentance, and hosting a guest. «یمارون» means insistence on doubt, and «مشفق» (mushfiq) derives from shafaqat, meaning fear accompanied by careful attention (or solicitude). Haqq (Truth) is an attribute mentioned in the Quran for various matters: Allah. «ذلك بان اللّه هوالحقّ»(13) (That is because Allah is the Truth.) The Prophet. «ارسلناك بالحقّ»(14) (We have sent you with the Truth.) The Quran. «انزل الكتاب بالحقّ»(15) (He sent down the Book with the Truth.) The Call (Da'wah). «له دعوة الحقّ»(16) (To Him is the call of Truth.) The Resurrection (Ma'ad). «یعلمون انها الحقّ» (They know that it is the Truth.) The Weighing. «والوزن یومئذ الحقّ»(17) (And the weighing on that Day will be the Truth.)

13) Luqman, 30. 14) Al-Baqarah, 119. 15) Ash-Shura, 17. 16) Ar-Ra'd, 14. 17) Al-A'raf, 8.

Messages

1- Our haste plays no role in Divine planning and is inappropriate. «یستعجل»

2- Stating two viewpoints and comparing them is a factor for growth and better selection. «لا یؤمنون بها والّذین آمنوا»

3- Faith must be based on knowledge. «والّذین آمنوا... یعلمون...»

4- Belief in the Resurrection causes fear of it. (Because the Day of Judgment is the place where terrifying events occur.) «آمنوا مشفقون»

5- Knowledge, faith, and fear are beneficial together; if one of them is missing, it is ineffective (just as Iblis, Bal'am Ba'ura, and Pharaoh had knowledge but lacked faith and fear). «والّذین آمنوا مشفقون منها و یعلمون انّها الحقّ»

6- The very principle of doubt regarding the Resurrection is misguidance, and creating doubt and insisting upon it in others is a far-off misguidance. «ضلال بعید»

7- Persistence and continuation in dispute are criticized. «یمارون فى الساعة»

Ayahs

ٱللَّهُ لَطِيفٌۢ بِعِبَادِهِۦ يَرْزُقُ مَن يَشَآءُ ۖ وَهُوَ ٱلْقَوِىُّ ٱلْعَزِيزُ (۱۹)
Allah is all-attentive to His servants. He provides for whomever He wishes, and He is the All-strong, the All-mighty. (19)
مَن كَانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ نَزِدْ لَهُۥ فِى حَرْثِهِۦ ۖ وَمَن كَانَ يُرِيدُ حَرْثَ ٱلدُّنْيَا نُؤْتِهِۦ مِنْهَا وَمَا لَهُۥ فِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ مِن نَّصِيبٍ (۲٠)
Whoever desires the tillage of the Hereafter, We will enhance for him his tillage, and whoever desires the tillage of the world, We will give it to him, but he will have no share in the Hereafter. (20)

Key Points

The meaning of Lutf (Grace/Subtlety) encompasses gentleness and precision. Allah is Latif (Subtle/Gracious), meaning He encompasses the minutiae of all affairs and executes every task with ease and facility. (18) The desire for the Hereafter is a sign of: broad vision, detachment from the world, faith in divine promises, giving the color of permanence to transient things, and, in a single phrase, a sign of intellect and the best choice. In Ayah 20, man is likened to a farmer, his action to cultivation, his intention to the seed, and the world and the Hereafter to the farm and the place of harvest. The Hereafter is more important than the world because: 1. The seeker of the Hereafter receives expansion and growth. «نزد له فى حرثه» 2. Seekers of the Hereafter receive a small share of the world, but seekers of the world receive no share of the Hereafter. «ما له فى الاخرة من نصیب» 3. Seekers of the Hereafter achieve all their goals. «نزد له فى حرثه» But seekers of the world only achieve a fraction of their goals. «نؤته منها»

18) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Unlike creatures, whose subtle ones are not strong and whose strong ones are not subtle, Allah is both Subtle (Latif) and Strong (Qawī). «لطیف... القوى»

2- The power of Allah is never defeated. «القوى العزیز»

3- Divine sustenance arises from the grace (lutf) of Allah. «لطیف بعباده یرزق»

4- The power and grace of Allah guarantee the provision of sustenance to people. «لطیف بعباده یرزق... و هو القوى»

5- Man is free in choosing his path. «من كان یرید... من كان یرید...»

6- The value of human action depends on his near and distant goals and his intention. «یرید حرث الاخرة - یرید حرث الدنیا»

7- All gifts are from Allah. «نزد له - نؤته منها» And the bestowal of Allah is commensurate with man's intention and choice. «من كان یرید.... من كان یرید»

8- Man's role is only in selection and decision; the extent of success is related to the Will of Allah. «نزد له - نوته منها»

9- Making the world the ultimate goal leads to complete deprivation from the Hereafter. «یرث حرث الدنیا... ما له فى الآخرة من نصیب»

Ayah

أَمْ لَهُمْ شُرَكَـٰٓؤُا۟ شَرَعُوا۟ لَهُم مِّنَ ٱلدِّينِ مَا لَمْ يَأْذَنۢ بِهِ ٱللَّهُ ۚ وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةُ ٱلْفَصْلِ لَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۗ وَإِنَّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (۲۱)
Do they have deities [besides Allah] who have ordained for them a religion not permitted by Allah? Were it not for a [prior] conclusive word, judgement would have been made between them, and indeed a painful punishment awaits the wrongdoers. (21)

Key Points

Imam Ali عليه السلام says to his son: «لو كان لربّك شریك لاَتَتك رسله» (19) If your Lord had a partner, messengers from Him would have come to you. This Ayah states the same meaning: Have the partners you imagine for Allah presented a school of thought or a religion to you? Imam Zayn al-'Abidin عليه السلام says regarding the divine respites: «كان جزائى منك فى اول ما عصیتك النار» (20) O God, my punishment for the first time I disobeyed You was the Fire (but You granted me respite).

(19) Nahj al-Balaghah, Letter 31. (20) Sahifah Sajjadiyyah, Supplication 16.

Messages

1- Man is in need of law, and only Allah can fulfill this need, not others. «ام لهم شركاء شرعوا لهم»

2- The true Divine Law (Shari'ah) is realized only with divine permission, and without His permission, nothing has a legal or divine aspect. «ما لم یأذن به اللّه»

3- Innovation (bid'ah) in religion and the legislation of laws without Allah's permission is polytheism (shirk) towards Him. «شركاء شرعوا لهم من الدین ما لم یأذن به اللّه»

4- It is the definite tradition (Sunnah) of Allah that all people should have a respite to show their true nature. «و لولا كلمة الفصل»

5- Granting respite to the deviants is a divine favor, so that perhaps they may consider repentance or faith; were it not for this favor, man would face the punishment of destruction with his very first sin. «لولا كلمة الفصل لقضى بینهم»

6- Polytheism (shirk) is oppression (zulm), and the polytheist is an oppressor (zalim). «ام لهم شركاء... انّ الظالمین»

Ayah

تَرَى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ مُشْفِقِينَ مِمَّا كَسَبُوا۟ وَهُوَ وَاقِعٌۢ بِهِمْ ۗ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ فِى رَوْضَاتِ ٱلْجَنَّاتِ ۖ لَهُم مَّا يَشَآءُونَ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَضْلُ ٱلْكَبِيرُ (۲۲)
When it is about to befall them, You will see the wrongdoers fearful because of what they have earned; but those who have faith and do righteous deeds will be in the gardens of paradise: they will have whatever they wish near their Lord. That is the mighty grace. (22)

Key Points

Rawḍah (روضة) refers to a place that has abundant water and trees.

Messages

1- The evil deeds of man bring him nothing but Hellfire. «مشفقین مما كسبوا»

2- Fear of punishment on the Day of Judgment is of no benefit. «مشفقین... وهو واقع بهم»

3- In Paradise, material and spiritual pleasures accompany one another. «روضات... عند ربّهم»

4- Worldly deprivations and the avoidance of forbidden things will be compensated for in time. «ما یشاؤن» (All material and spiritual desires of the believers are provided in Paradise.)

5- Although the inhabitants of Paradise are people of faith and good deeds, all those blessings are due to Divine favor, not merely the recompense for their actions. «الفضل الكبیر»

6- Nothing is expected from the Great God except the Great Favor. «هوالفضل الكبیر»

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ ٱلَّذِى يُبَشِّرُ ٱللَّهُ عِبَادَهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ ۗ قُل لَّآ أَسْـَٔلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْرًا إِلَّا ٱلْمَوَدَّةَ فِى ٱلْقُرْبَىٰ ۗ وَمَن يَقْتَرِفْ حَسَنَةً نَّزِدْ لَهُۥ فِيهَا حُسْنًا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ غَفُورٌ شَكُورٌ (۲۳)
Such is the good news that Allah gives to His servants who have faith and do righteous deeds! Say, ‘I do not ask you any reward for it except the love of [my] relatives.’ Whoever performs a good deed, We shall enhance its goodness for him. Indeed Allah is all-forgiving, all-appreciative. (23)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, we read that the reward for believing doers of good is the Gardens of Paradise, the attainment of all their desires, and the great favor of their Lord. This Ayah speaks of the reward for the one who, through his guidance, has led hundreds of millions of people to these Gardens and desires—that is, the Prophet of Islam. If the gift of a single flower requires thanks and reward, what reward will the salvation of humanity require? By looking at the Quran, we see that the slogan of all the Prophets was that they sought no reward except from their Lord. In Surah Ash-Shu'ara, from Ayah 109 to Ayah 127, the words of Noah, Hud, Salih, Lot, and Shu'ayb (علیهم السلام), and in Surah Saba, Ayah 47, the words of the Prophet of Islam are: My wage and reward is only upon Allah. «ان أجرى الاّ على اللّه» Of course, there is no doubt that the Prophets meant that they did not want material reward, but they wanted your obedience and guidance, because after all of them negated the request for a wage in Surah Ash-Shu'ara, they advised the people to have piety and obey Allah. «فاتقوا اللّه و اطیعون» Thus, the Prophets do not seek material reward, but they desire a spiritual wage and the guidance of the people, which is actually for the benefit of the people themselves. This is similar to a teacher telling his student: I do not want any wage from you, and my only reward is that you study, which in reality is a reward for the student.

What is the reward for the Prophethood of the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله)? The Prophet of Islam also did not request a material wage from the people, but He was commanded several times by Allah with the word "قل" (Say) to request the spiritual wage, which is for the benefit of the people themselves. «قل ما سألتكم من أجر فهو لكم»(21)

This spiritual wage is also mentioned in two expressions. Once He says: I do not want any wage except that whoever wills may choose the path of Allah, «الاّ من شاء ان یتّخذ الى ربّه سبیلا»(22) and once in the Ayah under discussion, where He says: «لا اسئلكم علیه اجرا الاّ المودّة فى القربى» (I do not ask you for any reward for it except the love of [my] near kin (Al-Qurba)).

Therefore, the reward for the Prophethood is two things: 1. Choosing the path of Allah, and 2. The love of the near kin (Mawaddah al-Qurba). It is interesting that in both expressions, the word «الاّ» (except/only) is used, meaning my reward is only this specific item.

With a little reflection, we understand that the path of Allah and the love of the Ahl al-Bayt must be one and the same, because if they were two, it would be a contradiction. That is, one cannot say: I only study in the summers, and then say again: I only study in the winters. Because the exclusive item must be one thing. The Prophet of Islam is commanded by Allah once to tell the people: My reward is only choosing the path of Allah, and another time He is commanded to say: My reward is only the love of the near kin. In reality, these two requests must be one. That is, the path of Allah must be the same as the love of the near kin.

Furthermore, love (Mawaddah) is inseparable from two things: 1. Recognition and knowledge (Ma'rifah), because unless a person knows someone, he cannot love him. 2. Obedience, because love without obedience is a kind of pretense, hypocrisy, falsehood, and flattery. Therefore, those who take their instructions from sources other than the Ahl al-Bayt of the Prophet (علیهم السلام) have not taken the path of Allah. This is from the perspective of the Quran.

Now, from the perspective of reason (Aql). The reward must be commensurate and equal in weight to the action. Prophethood has no equal weight except for the Imamate, which is its continuation. The reward for Prophethood is the continuation of guidance. The reward for one infallible person is entrusting the task to another infallible person. The reward for a just person is entrusting his efforts to another just person.

Reason says: As long as there is favor, there must be gratitude. If the Prophet's favor has reached us today and we have been guided to Islam, we must pay the reward for His Prophethood. If the reward for Prophethood is the love of the near kin (Mawaddah al-Qurba), then there must be a near kin today toward whom we show love and obedience. Yes, today too, we must have love for Imam Mahdi (عليه السلام) and be obedient to Him. Can we say that the Muslims of early Islam were commanded to pay the reward of Prophethood and show love toward the near kin, but today's Muslims either do not have this duty or there is no near kin to love and pay the reward of Prophethood to?

Of course, love for Imam Mahdi (عليه السلام) during the Occultation (Ghaybah) means acting upon His messages and following the path of those He entrusted us to, namely, the just jurists (fuqaha) free from whims and desires.

Human reason understands that since the love of the near kin has been established as the reward for a Prophet who guided hundreds of millions of people to guidance, happiness, and the great Divine favor, the near kin and those who are the object of this love must be the most excellent and infallible of mankind. This is because the love of a sinner can never be the reward for an infallible Prophet. It cannot be believed that the love of sinful individuals is obligatory upon the Muslims of the world throughout history, yet no sect of Muslims (other than the Shi'a) considers their leaders infallible, and no individual or group has ever narrated a sin for the infallible Imams or named a teacher for those great personalities.

Reason says: Placing the hand of humanity in the hand of a non-infallible person is not only an injustice to humanity but an injustice to the entire existence. This is because existence was created for man (all the Ayahs «خلق لكم», «سخّر لكم», and «متاعا لكم» indicate that existence is for man), and man was created for true spiritual perfection and becoming Godly. Is entrusting this man, with those lofty goals, to non-infallible leaders not an injustice to him and an injustice to existence?

If, in the narrations, the infallible leader and His Wilayah (guardianship) are recognized as the foundation and basis of religion, «بنى الاسلام على خمس... الولایة»(23) and if Imam Ali (عليه السلام) is introduced as the divider of people between Paradise and Hell, «قسیم الجنّة و النار»(24) and if prayer without Wilayah is not accepted,(25) and if the love of the Ahl al-Bayt is a good deed (hasanah),(26) and if visiting and seeking intercession through them is recommended, all of this is due to that alchemical essence of love (Mawaddah).

Zamakhshari and Fakhr al-Razi, who are among the great scholars of the Ahl al-Sunnah, have stated in their commentaries that the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله) said:

«مَن مات على حبّ آل محمّد مات شهیداً» Whoever dies upon the love of the Family of Muhammad dies a martyr. «مَن مات على حبّ آل محمّد مات تائباً» Whoever dies upon the love of the Family of Muhammad dies repentant. «مَن مات على حبّ آل محمّد مات مستكمل الایمان» Whoever dies upon the love of the Family of Muhammad dies with perfected faith. «مَن مات على حبّ آل محمّد مات على السنة والجماعة» Whoever dies upon the love of the Family of Muhammad dies according to the Sunnah and the practice of the Prophet.

Now the question arises: Can love without obedience be equal in weight to martyrdom, forgiveness, and perfect faith?

In these same commentaries, under this Ayah, a Hadith from the Messenger of Allah (صلى الله عليه وآله) is cited:

«ألا و مَن مات على بغض آل محمّد جاء یوم القیامة مكتوب بین عینیه آیس من رحمة اللّه», Beware, whoever dies with hatred and malice toward the Family of Muhammad will come on the Day of Resurrection with written between his eyes: He is deprived and despairing of the Mercy of Allah. «ألا و مَن مات على بغض آل محمّد مات كافرا» Beware, whoever dies with hatred toward the Family of Muhammad dies a disbeliever. «ألا و مَن مات على بغض آل محمّد لم یشم رائحة الجنة» Whoever dies with hatred toward the Family of Muhammad will not smell the fragrance of Paradise.

Fakhr al-Razi states in his commentary: When the Ayah of Mawaddah was revealed, the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) was asked: Who are the near kin (Qurba) whose love is obligatory upon us? The Prophet replied: Ali, Fatimah, and their children. He then adds that the Prophet said: «فاطمة بضعة منّى یؤذینى ما یؤذیها» Fatimah is a part of me; whatever harms her harms me. And the punishment for those who harm the Messenger of Allah is stated in the Quran as follows: «انّ الّذین یؤذون اللّه و رسوله لعنهم اللّه فى الدنیا و الآخرة و اعدّ لهم عذاباً مهیناً»(27)

In a Hadith, we read that Imam Hassan (عليه السلام), regarding the phrase «و مَن یقترف حسنة نزد له فیها حسناً» (and whoever earns a good deed, We will increase for him good therein), said: «اقتراف الحسنة مودّتنا اهل البیت»(28) Acquiring the good deed is our love, the Ahl al-Bayt.

21) Saba, 47. 22) Al-Furqan, 57. 23) Kafi, Vol. 2, p. 18. 24) Bihar, Vol. 7, p. 186. 25) Bihar, Vol. 27, p. 167. 26) Bihar, Vol. 43, p. 362. 27) Al-Ahzab, 57. 28) Tafsir Namuneh and Safi.

Messages

  1. The glad tidings of Allah are very great and immense. «روضات الجنّات - ما یشاؤون - فضل كبیر - ذلك الّذى یبشّر اللّه عباده» (Gardens of Paradise - whatever they desire - great favor - that is what Allah gives glad tidings to His servants).
  2. The sign of Allah's servant is faith and righteous deeds. «عباده الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات» (His servants who have believed and done righteous deeds).
  3. The secret to receiving all those blessings and glad tidings is servitude to Allah, faith, and good deeds. «عباده الّذین آمنوا و عملوا...»
  4. The love of the near kin (Mawaddah al-Qurba) is a clear manifestation of faith and righteous deeds. «یبشر اللّه عباده الّذین آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات قل لا اسئلكم... الا المودة فى القربى»
  5. Requesting a wage from one's own mouth is difficult, so the Prophet is commanded by Allah to announce His wage to the people. «قل لا اسئلكم...»
  6. Love without knowledge is impossible. (Thus, the reward of Prophethood, before anything else, is the recognition of the Prophet's Ahl al-Bayt, and then their love.) «الاّ المودّة فى القربى»
  7. Love without obedience is hypocrisy and flattery. (Thus, the love of the near kin means obedience to them.) «الاّ المودّة فى القربى»
  8. The near kin (Qurba) are infallible, because the love of a sinner cannot be the reward for Prophethood. «الاّ المودّة فى القربى»
  9. The love that is necessarily accompanied by knowledge and obedience is established only in the Ahl al-Bayt. (The word «فى» (in) is the symbol that the locus of love is only the Ahl al-Bayt.) «فى القربى»
  10. The love of the near kin (Mawaddah fil Qurba) is a path to acquiring good deeds. «الاّ المودّة فى القربى و مَن یقترف حسنة»
  11. The love of the Ahl al-Bayt (علیهم السلام) is the most prominent good deed. «المودّة فى القربى و من یقترف حسنة»
  12. The love of the near kin is a good deed (hasanah). «مَن یقترف حسنة»
  13. The love of the near kin is a cause for receiving greater reward. «نزد له فیها حسناً» (We will increase for him good therein).
  14. The love of the Prophet's Ahl al-Bayt is the basis for receiving forgiveness. «الاّ المودّة فى القربى... انّ اللّه غفور»
  15. Allah appreciates those who fulfill their duty regarding the love of the Ahl al-Bayt. «انّ اللّه غفور شكور» (Indeed, Allah is Forgiving, Appreciative).

Ayah

أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا ۖ فَإِن يَشَإِ ٱللَّهُ يَخْتِمْ عَلَىٰ قَلْبِكَ ۗ وَيَمْحُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْبَـٰطِلَ وَيُحِقُّ ٱلْحَقَّ بِكَلِمَـٰتِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌۢ بِذَاتِ ٱلصُّدُورِ (۲٤)
Do they say, ‘He has fabricated a lie against Allah’? If so, should Allah wish He would set a seal on your heart, and Allah will efface the falsehood and confirm the truth with His words. Indeed He knows well what is in the breasts. (24)

Key Points

Just as the deviants said regarding the issue of prophethood (Risalah): "The Prophet is a human being like us, and his claim of prophethood is a fabrication against Allah," they made the same accusation regarding the issue of Imamate. This Ayah states: If the Prophet were to fabricate a lie against Allah, We would be wrathful towards him and seal his heart, similar to where He says: «لو تقوّل علینا بعض الاقاویل لاخذناه منه بالیمین ثمّ لقطعنا منه الوتین»(29) (If he were to falsely attribute some sayings to Us, We would seize him by the right hand, then We would cut his life-vein.) «ذات الصدور» (Dhat as-Sudur) means the thoughts that have taken possession of the chests (hearts).

29) Al-Haqqah, 44-46.

Messages

1- Some who did not want to consider the affection (Mawaddah) for the Ahl al-Bayt as part of the message (Risalah) said: "The Prophet has lied against Allah by saying that the reward for my message is the affection for the near kin (Qurba)." «ام یقولون افترى»

2- To clarify the truth, doubts must be raised and answered. «ام یقولون... فان یشأ اللّه»

3- Fabrication is not acceptable from anyone. «یختم على قلبك» (If the Prophet were to attribute the slightest lie to Allah, his falsehood would be exposed, and he would be deprived of the blessing of receiving revelation.)

4- Sealing the heart is the greatest divine punishment, as it is the retribution for the greatest sin. «افترى... یختم»

5- The heart is the center for receiving revelation. «یختم على قلبك» (Just as He says elsewhere: The revelation descended upon your heart. «نزل به الروح الامین على قلبك»(30))

6- The tradition (Sunnah) of Allah is to erase falsehood and establish the permanence of truth. «یمح اللّه... یحقّ الحق»

7- Allah consoles His Prophet with the promise of the destruction of falsehood and the victory of truth. «یقولون افترى... یمح اللّه الباطل و یحقّ الحقّ»

8- The Prophet has not only refrained from fabricating a lie against Allah, but he has not even entertained the thought of fabrication. This is because Allah has complete knowledge of the secrets hidden within the chests (hearts). «انّ اللّه علیم بذات الصدور» 30) Ash-Shu'ara, 194.

Ayah

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى يَقْبَلُ ٱلتَّوْبَةَ عَنْ عِبَادِهِۦ وَيَعْفُوا۟ عَنِ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ وَيَعْلَمُ مَا تَفْعَلُونَ (۲٥)
It is He who accepts the repentance of His servants, and excuses their misdeeds and knows what you do. (25)

Messages

1- The acceptance of the repentance of sinners and their forgiveness is solely in the power of Allah. «و هو الّذى»

2- There is no dead end in Islam, and the path of return is always open. «یقبل التوبة»

3- Allah forgives all sins. «یعفوا عن السیّئات» (The word «سیّئات» is a plural accompanied by alif and lam, meaning all sins.)

4- Allah encourages the misguided to repent by promising forgiveness. «یقبل التوبة... یعفوا عن السیّئات»

5- Repentance must be accompanied by a change in behavior and speech; otherwise, it is a form of pretense and hypocrisy, which Allah knows. «و یعلم ما تفعلون»

Ayah

وَيَسْتَجِيبُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَيَزِيدُهُم مِّن فَضْلِهِۦ ۚ وَٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ شَدِيدٌ (۲٦)
He answers [the supplications of] those who have faith and do righteous deeds and enhances them out of His grace. But as for the faithless, there is a severe punishment for them. (26)

Key Points

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said regarding this Ayah: The prayer (du'a) of a believer for his religious brother is answered, because Allah says: Due to the affection you have for your religious brother, I grant what you asked for to you and to the one for whom you prayed. (31) In a narration from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, we read: The meaning of the phrase "و یزیدهم من فضله" (and He increases them from His bounty) is the right of intercession (shafa'ah) granted to believers concerning those who, although destined for Hellfire, had rendered a service to the believers. (32)

31) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 32) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Allah accepts the prayer (du'a) of righteous believers. "و یستجیب"

2- The condition for the acceptance of prayer is faith and righteous deeds. "یستجیب الّذین آمنوا وعملوا الصالحات"

3- Reward that is abundant and beyond expectation is a manifestation of Divine bounty. "و یزیدهم من فضله"

4- Threat and encouragement are necessary side by side. "یقبل التوبة - یعفو عن السیئات - یستجیب - یزیدهم من فضله - والكافرون لهم عذاب شدید"

Ayah

۞ وَلَوْ بَسَطَ ٱللَّهُ ٱلرِّزْقَ لِعِبَادِهِۦ لَبَغَوْا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَـٰكِن يُنَزِّلُ بِقَدَرٍ مَّا يَشَآءُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ بِعِبَادِهِۦ خَبِيرٌۢ بَصِيرٌ (۲۷)
Were Allah to expand the provision for [all] His servants, they would surely create havoc on the earth. But He sends down in a [precise] measure whatever He wishes. Indeed, He is all-aware, all-seeing about His servants. (27)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, Allah promised the believers acceptance (of their prayers). Now, the question arises: why are some prayers of the believers for the expansion of sustenance (rizq) not answered? In response, it must be said: the acceptance of prayer is within the framework of Divine Wisdom. Sometimes, the expansion of sustenance is a factor leading to heedlessness of Allah and causes rebellion (tughyan) and transgression. How many are those whose wealth has led to their destruction! Another question is: if the expansion of sustenance causes rebellion, then why did He grant expansion to some groups, and they subsequently rebelled? In response, we say: the Divine programs and traditions (Sunan) are numerous and have stages. Just as controlling sustenance to prevent corruption is a general tradition (Sunnah), expanding sustenance and granting respite to the affluent is also a tradition used for testing. Therefore, the general and universal law is the control of sustenance, but sometimes expansion is granted to test people. (33) It should be noted that sometimes the shortage of sustenance is due to human laziness (self-indulgence), which is a separate matter.

33) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Just as Divine gifts are a grace (lutf), sometimes deprivations are also a grace. «لو بسط اللّه الرزق لعباده لبغوا»

2- Divine favor is distributed based on expediency and wisdom, not based on human desires. «و لو بسط اللّه...»

3- Healthy social relations depend on a moderate life, limited to necessity. «لو بسط... لبغوا»

4- Believers should not harbor ill-will (su'u al-zann) toward Allah because of financial hardship. «و لو بسط اللّه»

5- Excessive welfare (affluence) is the ground for rebellion (tughyan). «لو بسط... لبغوا»

6- Divine Will (Mashiyyah) is based on wisdom. «ما یشاء انه بعباده خبیر بصیر»

7- The sustenance (rizq) of people is calculated and measured. «ینزّل بقدر»

Ayahs

وَهُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُنَزِّلُ ٱلْغَيْثَ مِنۢ بَعْدِ مَا قَنَطُوا۟ وَيَنشُرُ رَحْمَتَهُۥ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْوَلِىُّ ٱلْحَمِيدُ (۲۸)
It is He who sends down the rain after they have been despondent, and unfolds His mercy, and He is the Guardian, the All-laudable. (28)
وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ خَلْقُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَثَّ فِيهِمَا مِن دَآبَّةٍ ۚ وَهُوَ عَلَىٰ جَمْعِهِمْ إِذَا يَشَآءُ قَدِيرٌ (۲۹)
Among His signs is the creation of the heavens and the earth and whatever creatures He has scattered in them, and He is able to gather them whenever He wishes. (29)

Key Points

  • Ghauth (غیث) refers to beneficial rain. However, matar (مطر) refers to any type of rain (whether beneficial or harmful). As He says: «اُمطرت مطر السوء» (34). Qunut (قنوط) means despair, and bathth (بَثّ) means scattering or spreading.

34) Al-Furqan, 40.

Messages

1- In despair there is much hope; the end of the dark night is white (dawn). «ینزّل الغیث من بعد ما قنطوا»

2- The only refuge for humanity in difficulties and despair is Allah. «و هو الّذى... من بعد ما قنطوا»

3- Rain is a manifestation of Divine Mercy. «ینزل الغیث... و ینشر رحمته»

4- The scarcity of rain in some instances is to prevent human beings from transgressing. «و لو بسط اللّه الرزق لعباده لبغوا... ینزل الغیث من بعد ما قنطوا»

5- Praiseworthy and eternal guardianship (Wilayah) belongs exclusively to Allah. «و هو الولىّ الحمید»

6- The creation of the heavens, the earth, and the creatures (dābbah) is a part of the Divine Signs. «من آیاته»

7- Allah’s power in the initial creation is a sign of His power to establish the Resurrection (Qiyamah). «خلق السموات والارض... و هو على جمعهم... قدیر»

8- There are also living creatures in the heavens. «بثّ فیهما من دابّة»

9- The scattering of animals across different regions of the earth is one of the blessings. (If all sheep were in one region and all cows in another, life would be very difficult and costly for humans.) «بثّ فیهما من دابّة»

10- The determination of the time of the Resurrection is solely in His control. «اذا یشاء»

Ayahs

وَمَآ أَصَـٰبَكُم مِّن مُّصِيبَةٍ فَبِمَا كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ وَيَعْفُوا۟ عَن كَثِيرٍ (۳٠)
Whatever affliction that may visit you is because of what your hands have earned, and He excuses many [an offense of yours]. (30)
وَمَآ أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۖ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مِن وَلِىٍّ وَلَا نَصِيرٍ (۳۱)
You cannot frustrate [Allah] on the earth, and you do not have besides Allah any guardian or helper. (31)

Key Points

Question: If calamities are due to our own actions, then what is the justification for the calamities that befall the friends (Awliya) of Allah? Answer: The programs and Divine traditions (Sunan) are numerous. One of these traditions is making the sinful human taste the bitter flavor of his actions, which is mentioned in this Ayah. However, another Divine tradition is the growth of people amidst successive events and trials. Therefore, the bitter events that occur for the Infallibles (Ma'sumin) are for their spiritual growth, the attainment of higher ranks (darajah), and for them to serve as role models for others. We read in a Hadith: «البلاء للظالم ادب و للمؤمن امتحان و للاولیاء درجة»(35) Bitter events are a means of discipline for the oppressor (zalim), a means of testing for the believer (mu'min), and a means of greater proximity (qurb) for the friends of Allah (Awliya). Similar to this Ayah is Ayah 41 of Surah Ar-Rum, which states: «ظهر الفساد فى البرّ و البحر بما كسبت ایدى الناس لیذیقهم بعض الّذى عملوا لعلهم یرجعون» Corruption has appeared in the land and the sea because of what the hands of people have earned, so that He may make them taste some of what they have done, that they may return (to righteousness).

35) Bihar, Vol. 80, p. 189.

Messages

1- There is a relationship between human behavior and the bitter and sweet events of life. «ما اصابكم... فبما كسبت ایدیكم»

2- Human problems are only the reaction to a portion of his transgressions, not all of them, because Allah overlooks many of man's mistakes. «و یعفو عن كثیر»

3- Calamities have a warning aspect; if they were purely retribution, there would be no forgiveness involved. «و یعفو عن كثیر»

4- The smoke of human crimes goes into his own eyes and does not harm Allah. «و ما انتم بمعجزین»

5- Humans cannot bring all factors under their control and escape the consequences of their sins. «و ما انتم بمعجزین»

6- The motive for many transgressions is to gain a helper and supporter, whereas the true Helper is Allah. «و ما لكم من دون اللّه من ولىّ ولا نصیر»

Ayahs

وَمِنْ ءَايَـٰتِهِ ٱلْجَوَارِ فِى ٱلْبَحْرِ كَٱلْأَعْلَـٰمِ (۳۲)
Among His signs are the ships [that run] on the sea [appearing] like landmarks. (32)
إِن يَشَأْ يُسْكِنِ ٱلرِّيحَ فَيَظْلَلْنَ رَوَاكِدَ عَلَىٰ ظَهْرِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّكُلِّ صَبَّارٍ شَكُورٍ (۳۳)
If He wishes He stills the wind, whereat they remain standstill on its surface. There are indeed signs in that for every patient and grateful [servant]. (33)

Key Points

"Al-Juwār" (الجوار) is the plural of "Jāriyah" (جاریة), meaning a ship that is sailing and moving. The intent of "kal-a'lām" (كالاعلام) is either large, gigantic ships or flowing icebergs. One must not overlook the role of wind in nature. The breathing of living creatures, the movement of ships, the transfer of clouds, the fertilization of plants, and the moderation of heat and cold are all dependent on the flow of wind. Today, wind is considered a source of energy. The word "ṣabbār" (صبّار) has been used four times in the Holy Quran, and in every instance, it is followed by the word "shakūr" (شكور). This indicates that patience (ṣabr) and gratitude (shukr) have a special role when placed side by side. In a hadith from the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله, it is stated that faith has two branches: one branch is patience (ṣabr) and the other branch is gratitude (shukr). (36)

36) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- The proportionality between seawater and the ship in terms of weight and volume is among the signs of Divine power. «و من آیاته الجوار فى البحر» (The fact that gigantic ships do not sink into the depths of the seas is due to the differences Allah has placed between bodies and masses.)

2- The value of a blessing is recognized when it is lost. «ان یشا یسكن الریح»

3- What we see as one sign (āyah) is seen as multiple signs by patient researchers. «لآیات لكلّ صبّار»

4- Nature and its manifestations, such as water and wind, are signs (āyāt) for those who are patient (ṣābir) regarding the hardships of the world and grateful (shākir) regarding its prosperity. «لآیات لكلّ صبّار»

Ayahs

أَوْ يُوبِقْهُنَّ بِمَا كَسَبُوا۟ وَيَعْفُ عَن كَثِيرٍ (۳٤)
Or He wrecks them because of what they have earned, and He excuses many [an offense]. (34)
وَيَعْلَمَ ٱلَّذِينَ يُجَـٰدِلُونَ فِىٓ ءَايَـٰتِنَا مَا لَهُم مِّن مَّحِيصٍ (۳٥)
Let those who dispute Our signs know that there is no escape for them. (35)

Key Points

"Yūbiqhunna" (یوبقهن) is derived from ībaq (ایباق), meaning to destroy, and here it is a metaphor for drowning. "Maḥīṣ" (محیص) is derived from ḥayṣ (حیص), meaning a path of return or escape (salvation). The deviants, instead of reflecting, dispute concerning the signs of Allah. In the previous verses, He said: "لایات لكل صبار شكور" (Surely there are signs in this for every patient and grateful person), and in this verse, He says: "یجادلون فى آیات اللّه" (They dispute concerning the signs of Allah).

Messages

1- We should never consider ourselves safe. Allah can destroy us, both on the sea and on the land, because of our actions. "او یوبقهن" (Or He destroys them).

2- Only some sins are sufficient for our destruction. "یوبقهن... و یعف عن كثیر" (He destroys them... and pardons much).

3- The grace of Allah prevails over His wrath. "یعف عن كثیر" (He pardons much).

4- Punishment and pardon are in the hand of Allah. "یوبقهن - یعف" (He destroys - He pardons).

5- Those who interpret and materially analyze natural phenomena instead of paying attention to Divine power will have no escape when the wrath of Allah arrives. "یجادلون فى آیاتنا... ما لهم من محیص" (They dispute concerning Our signs... they have no place of escape).

6- Stubborn disbelievers are deprived of Divine pardon. "و یعف عن كثیر... ما لهم من محیص" (And He pardons much... they have no place of escape).

Ayah

فَمَآ أُوتِيتُم مِّن شَىْءٍ فَمَتَـٰعُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ وَمَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌ وَأَبْقَىٰ لِلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَلَىٰ رَبِّهِمْ يَتَوَكَّلُونَ (۳٦)
Whatever you have been given are the wares of the life of this world, but what is with Allah is better and more lasting for those who have faith and who put their trust in their Lord (36)

Key Points

The previous Ayah spoke of dispute (jidal). It seems this Ayah states that the root of dispute is attachment to the life of this world, which is merely a fleeting provision.

Messages

  1. What we possess is not due to our skill, but rather His grace that He has bestowed upon us. «اوتیتم»
  2. What is in our possession is not something we should be pleased with or attached to. All existence is insignificant. «ما اوتیتم... فمتاع الحیاة الدنیا»
  3. The God for Whom both this world and the Hereafter belong, has designated only the Hereafter as belonging to Him. «ما عند اللّه»
  4. What the believer loses in this world, Allah gives him something better in the Resurrection. «ما عند اللّه خیر و ابقى»
  5. The world is given to everyone. «اوتیتم»
  6. The Hereafter is exclusive to the believing ones who rely (on Him). «للّذین آمنوا و على ربّهم یتوكّلون»
  7. The world is transient and the Hereafter is lasting. «ما عنداللّه... ابقى»
  8. Reliance (Tawakkul) is the constant practice of the believer. «یتوكّلون»

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ يَجْتَنِبُونَ كَبَـٰٓئِرَ ٱلْإِثْمِ وَٱلْفَوَٰحِشَ وَإِذَا مَا غَضِبُوا۟ هُمْ يَغْفِرُونَ (۳۷)
—those who avoid major sins and indecencies, and forgive when angered; (37)

Key Points

The previous Ayah discussed faith (Iman) and reliance (Tawakkul), both of which relate to the heart. This Ayah addresses the practical methods of the believers.

What are the major sins (Kabā'ir)? Imam Khomeini (قدس سره) states in Tahrir al-Wasilah regarding the conditions for the Imam of the congregational prayer (Imam Jama'at): The Imam Jama'at must abstain from major sins, which are: any sin for which a promise of punishment has been given in the Qur'an, or which has been severely condemned, or for which we have evidence that it is considered equal to or worse than known major sins, or which the intellect deems major, or which is considered major in the general view of the believers, or for which we have specific evidence of its being major.

He then enumerates examples, including: despair of Allah's mercy, falsely attributing lies to Allah and the Messenger, murder (Qatl), false accusation against a married woman, fleeing from battle, disobedience to parents ('Uqūq al-Wālidayn), severing kinship ties (Qat' al-Raḥim), sorcery (Siḥr), adultery (Zinā), sodomy (Liwāṭ), consuming the property of an orphan, concealing the truth (Kitmān al-Ḥaqq), false testimony, breaking covenants, drinking wine, exceeding limits in a will (Tajāwuz fi al-Waṣiyyah), usury (Ribā), gambling (Qimār), giving short measure, eating carrion and pork, aiding the oppressor and relying upon him, extravagance and waste (Isrāf wa Tabdhīr), withholding people's rights, lying, arrogance (Takabbur), betrayal (Khiyānah), backbiting (Ghaybah), tale-bearing (Sukhun Chīnī), treating Hajj lightly, abandoning prayer (Ṣalāt), withholding Zakat, persistence in minor sins, and engaging in vain amusements (Lahw wa La'b).

Messages

1- The condition for benefiting from superior and more lasting blessings, besides faith and reliance (Tawakkul), is abstaining from sins and controlling desires and anger. «و ما عنداللّه خیر و ابقى للّذین آمنوا و على ربّهم یتوكّلون و الّذین یجتنبون...»

2- The first step in reform is purification, followed by self-building and community-building. «یجتنبون كبائر الاثم»

3- The source of many sins is desire (Shahwah) and anger (Ghaḍab); first, He mentioned abstaining from desire, then abstaining from anger. «یجتنبون... الفواحش و اذا ما غضبوا»

4- We strengthen our relationship with Allah through Divine piety and abstaining from sins, and we strengthen our relationship with people through forgiveness when angry. «یجتنبون... یغفرون»

5- Perfections gain greater value when they become a person's constant practice and method. «یجتنبون... یغفرون» (The present tense form indicates permanence.)

6- The believer has mastery over his soul (Nafs). «یجتنبون... یغفرون»

7- Anger is an instinct, but controlling it is necessary. «غضبوا»

8- Believers themselves grant pardon when angry, not because of the mediation of others. «هم یغفرون»

Ayahs

وَٱلَّذِينَ ٱسْتَجَابُوا۟ لِرَبِّهِمْ وَأَقَامُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ وَأَمْرُهُمْ شُورَىٰ بَيْنَهُمْ وَمِمَّا رَزَقْنَـٰهُمْ يُنفِقُونَ (۳۸)
those who answer their Lord, maintain the prayer, and [conduct] their affairs by counsel among themselves, and they spend out of what We have provided them; (38)
وَٱلَّذِينَ إِذَآ أَصَابَهُمُ ٱلْبَغْىُ هُمْ يَنتَصِرُونَ (۳۹)
those who, when afflicted by oppression, defend themselves. (39)

Key Points

Talhah and Zubayr said to Imam Ali عليه السلام: We will be with you on the condition that you consult us in every matter, because our status is separate from others. The Imam replied: «نظرتُ فى كتاب اللّه و سنّة رسوله فامضیت ما دلاّنى علیه و اتبعته و لم احتج الى آرائكما فیه ولا رأى غیركما ولو وقع حكم لیس فى كتاب اللّه بیانه ولا فى السنّة برهانه و احتیج الى المشاورة فیه لشاورتكما فیه»(37) I look into the Book of God and the Sunnah of His Messenger, and I follow whatever is there, and I have no need for the opinion or consultation of you or others. However, if there is a matter for which I have no proof in the Book or the Sunnah, and consultation is needed, I will consult you.

In Ayah 38, the system of Shura (consultation) for managing the affairs of society is emphasized, and for this reason, this Surah is named Ash-Shura. We also, on this occasion, present a selection of narrations regarding consultation and Shura here. (38)

«شاور العلماء الصالحین» "Consult the righteous scholars." «واجعل مشورتك من یخاف اللّه» "Make those who fear Allah your consultants." «شاور المتّقین الّذین یؤثرون الاخرة على الدنیا» "Consult the pious who prefer the Hereafter over the world." «خیر من شاورت ذوى النهى والعلم و اولوا التجارب و الحزم» "The best people you consult are those possessing intellect, knowledge, experience, and prudence." «لا تدخلّن فى مشورتك بخیلاً و لا جباناً و لا حریصا» "Do not include a miser, a coward, or a greedy person in your consultation." «رأى الرجل على قدر تجربته» "The value of a man's opinion is commensurate with his experience." «أن یكون حرّاً متدیّناً صدیّقاً و ان تطلعه على سرّك» Consult those who are free (independent), religious, and truthful, and inform him of your secret (so he can provide a comprehensive and complete opinion). «مشاورة العاقل الناصح رشد و یمن و توفیق من اللّه» "Consultation with a wise, sincere advisor is guidance, blessing, and success granted by Allah." «شاور فى امورك من فیه خمس خصال...» "Consult in your affairs with someone who possesses five characteristics: intellect, knowledge, experience, sincerity (goodwill), and piety."

Question: Why is consultation with women forbidden in Letter 31 of Nahj al-Balaghah? Answer: Consultation is not dependent on gender, but rather on criteria and standards. Imam Ali عليه السلام says: «فانّ رأیهن الى افن» "For their opinion tends toward weakness," because a woman is emotional and sentimental, her opinion is usually not based on reasoning. Therefore, in another narration, He says: If you have experience of a woman's intellect and perfection, consult her. Thus, the reason for forbidding consultation with women was the weakness of the opinion, and if a man is also like this, he should not be consulted.

Question: Given that the Quran states: most people do not reflect, «اكثرهم لا یعقلون»(39), most of them do not know, «اكثرهم لا یعلمون»(40), many of them are wicked, «اكثرهم فاسقون»(41), and many are unwilling to submit to the truth, «اكثرهم للحق كارهون»(42), then why is consultation recommended? Answer: The Quran refers to the majority of people who are afflicted by polytheism, corruption, and desires, and it never means "the majority of the pious" or "the majority of the believers."

Benefits of Consultation: - It reduces the probability of error. - It develops talents. - It prevents despotism. - It prevents the envy of others. If we succeed through consultation, since others know that our success is due to their thought and consultation, they will not envy us. A child who grows up is never envied by his father, because the father sees the child's growth as a reflection of his own growth. - It brings divine aid, as it is in our religious culture that «یداللّه مع الجماعه» (The Hand of Allah is with the community). - Utilizing the opinions of others makes the plan mature and comprehensive. «من شاور الرجال شارك فى عقولها» (Whoever consults men shares in their intellects). - Consultation is a form of respect for people; an entirely new idea may not be gained through consultation, but it respects people's personalities. - It is a means of recognizing others. Until a man speaks, his faults and virtues remain hidden. Through consultation, one can recognize the scientific and intellectual level, commitment, and planning ability of individuals.

Question: Was the Prophet's consultation with the people merely superficial? Answer: Never. Because if He consulted and then acted contrary to the advice, He would not only have failed to respect the community, but He would have, in a way, wounded their spirits. The Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله consulted with the Muslims in the battles of Badr, Uhud, Khandaq, Hudaybiyyah, the wars against Banu Qurayzah and Banu Nadir, the Conquest of Mecca, and the Battle of Tabuk.

Consultation is for matters related to the people, but in affairs related to Allah, such as prophethood (bi'that), Imamate, and worship, there is no place for consultation. Prayer is a divine covenant, «الصلاة عهد اللّه» (Prayer is the covenant of Allah). The covenant of God must be fulfilled and requires no consultation. Imamate and the leadership of the community are also divine covenants, because when Prophet Abraham requested Allah that his descendants become leaders of the community, Allah said: Leadership and Imamate are My covenant, and granting it depends on My selection, not your prayer, and if a person commits oppression, he is not worthy of leadership, «لاینال عهدى الظالمین» (My covenant does not include the wrongdoers). Therefore, regarding the leadership of the community, we must submit to Him, just as the Quran states: «انى جاعلك للناس اماماً» (Indeed, I will make you an Imam for mankind). Imamate is by My choice.

Islam is a comprehensive and complete religion and has addressed all matters: - In doctrinal matters: «آمنوا و یتوكّلون» - In ethical matters: «یجتنبون، یغفرون» - In social matters: «شورى بینهم» - In devotional matters: «اقاموا الصلاة» - In economic matters: «ینفقون» - In political and military matters: «ینتصرون»

It is interesting that these attributes are presented in the form of the imperfect tense (present/future), which indicates continuity.

37) Sharh Nahj al-Balaghah by Ibn Abi al-Hadid, Vol. 7, p. 41. 38) Bihar [al-Anwar], Vol. 72, p. 105. 39) Al-Ma'idah, 103. 40) Al-An'am, 37. 41) At-Tawbah, 8. 42) Al-Mu'minun, 70.

Messages

1- Those devoted to prayer and spending (in charity) will enjoy the superior and eternal blessings of the Day of Judgment. «ما عنداللّه خیر و ابقى... والّذین استجابوا...»

2- Attention to the Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah causes man's devotion to fulfilling His commands. «استجابوا لربّهم»

3- Responding to the call of God must be through action, not mere claim. «استجابوا... اقاموا»

4- We must control instincts and remove obstacles so that the path of servitude to Allah is opened. «یجتنبون - یغفرون - استجابوا لربهم»

5- The believer is not despotic, is not isolated, and respects the opinions of others. «و امرهم شورى بینهم»

6- What is commanded is the establishment of prayer, not merely performing it. (Meaning the magnificent performance of prayer with all its conditions.) «اقاموا الصلاة»

7- Prayer is distinct from other acts of worship. (Although prayer is among the matters that lead to the Lord's response, it is mentioned separately.) «استجابوا لربّهم و اقاموا الصلاة»

8- Shura and consultation relate to the social affairs of the people, not religious rulings and commands. «امرهم شورى بینهم»

9- Do not seek opinions from outsiders. «بینهم»

10- Spending (in charity) is not limited to wealth; one must also help others with knowledge, reputation, and power. «ممّا رزقناهم»

11- The spender must know that what he possesses is not his own; it is the provision (rizq) of Allah. «رزقناهم»

12- Prayer eradicates arrogance; consultation eradicates despotism; and spending eradicates stinginess in society. «الصلاة، شورى، ینفقون»
