13- Defense is necessary, even if it requires seeking aid from believers, and silence and submission to oppression are forbidden. «ینتصرون»

14- Forgiveness and pardon are necessary towards one's own people, while seeking aid and defense are necessary against oppressors. «یغفرون - ینتصرون»

Ayahs

وَجَزَٰٓؤُا۟ سَيِّئَةٍ سَيِّئَةٌ مِّثْلُهَا ۖ فَمَنْ عَفَا وَأَصْلَحَ فَأَجْرُهُۥ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٤٠)
The requital of evil is an evil like it, so whoever excuses and conciliates, his reward lies with Allah. Indeed, He does not like the wrongdoers. (40)
وَلَمَنِ ٱنتَصَرَ بَعْدَ ظُلْمِهِۦ فَأُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ مَا عَلَيْهِم مِّن سَبِيلٍ (٤۱)
As for those who retaliate after being wronged, there is no ground for action against them. (41)

Key Points

The phrase "فأجره على اللّه" (His reward is upon Allah) appears twice in the Holy Qur'an: once in Surah An-Nisa, Ayah 100, which relates to sincere emigrants (Muhajirin), and once in this Ayah, which relates to forgiving people's misdeeds. Thus, it becomes clear that the reward for forgiveness and reconciliation is equal in weight to the reward for migration towards Allah (Hijrah ila Allah).

The Law of Equivalence (Mumaathalah) in Islam: Since the laws of Islam are based on intellect, nature (Fitrah), and justice, they explicitly present equality in various matters. We present a few examples here: * «فمن اعتدى علیكم فاعتدوا علیه بمثل ما اعتدى علیكم»(43) Whoever commits aggression against you, commit aggression against him in the same measure as he committed aggression against you. * «و لهنّ مثل الّذى علیهنّ بالمعروف»(44), And for women are rights similar to the duties incumbent upon them, according to what is recognized (as good). * «و مَن قتله منكم متعمّداً فجزاء مثل ما قتل من النعم»(45), And whoever among you kills game intentionally while in the state of Ihram, his penalty and expiation is the killing of a similar animal from the livestock. * «و ان عاقبتم فعاقبوا بمثل ما عوقبتم به»(46) And if you punish, then punish with the like of that with which you were punished.

Examples of Equivalence in Divine Traditions: * «مكروا و مكراللّه»(47) The enemies plotted, and Allah planned (counter-plotted). * «یخادعون اللّه و هو خادعهم»(48) (The hypocrites) seek to deceive Allah, while He is deceiving them. * «انما نحن مستهزؤون اللّه یستهزى ء بهم»(49) (They say:) We are only mocking (them); Allah mocks them. * «نسوا اللّه فانساهم»(50) They forgot Allah, so He caused them to forget themselves. * «فلمّا زاغوا ازاغ اللّه»(51) When they deviated, Allah caused their hearts to deviate. * «و ان عُدتم عُدنا»(52) And if you return (to sin), We will return (to punishment). * «للّذین احسنوا الحسنى وزیادة»(53) For those who have done good is the best (reward) and increase. * «رضى اللّه عنهم و رضوا عنه»(54) Allah is pleased with them, and they are pleased with Him. * Those who said in this world: «سواء علینا اوعظت ام لم تكن من الواعظین»(55) It is the same to us whether you advise us or are not among the advisers (do not tire yourself needlessly), will say on the Day of Resurrection: «سواء علینا اجزعنا ام صبرنا»(56) It is the same to us whether we show impatience or we endure patiently; there is no difference for us. * «مَن كان فى هذه اعمى فهو فى الآخرة اعمى»(57) Those who were blind in this world (from seeing the face of truth) will also be blind in the Hereafter. * «فاذكرونى اذكركم»(58) Remember Me, and I will remember you. * If Allah commands us: «صلّوا علیه و سلّموا تسلیما»(59) Send blessings upon the Prophet and greet him with a (becoming) salutation and submit to his command, in return, He commands the Prophet: «و صلّ علیهم»(60) Send blessings upon those who pay Zakat.

43) Al-Baqarah, 149. 44) Al-Baqarah, 228. 45) Al-Ma'idah, 95. 46) An-Nahl, 126. 47) Aal-i-'Imran, 54. 48) An-Nisa, 142. 49) Al-Baqarah, 14 - 15. 50) Al-Hashr, 19. 51) As-Saff, 5. 52) Al-Isra, 8. 53) Yunus, 26. 54) Al-Ma'idah, 119. 55) Ash-Shu'ara, 136. 56) Ibrahim, 21. 57) Al-Isra, 72. 58) Al-Baqarah, 152. 59) Al-Ahzab, 56. 60) At-Tawbah, 103.

Messages

1- Just retaliation in response to violence is permissible. «و جزاء سیّئة سیّئة مثلها»

2- Forgiveness and reconciliation by one who has the power to retaliate will result in a great reward. «فمن عفا و اصلح فاجره على اللّه»

3- Retribution (Qisas) and punishment must correspond to the crime (not differ from it, which would lead to excess or deficiency). «جزاء سیّئة سیّئة مثلها»

4- The prerequisite and foundation for reconciliation is forgiveness; under conditions of revenge and punishment, reconciliation has no basis. «فمن عفا و اصلح»

5- Forgiving the misdeeds of others is not enough; one must also reform (the situation/the person). «عفا و اصلح»

6- Legal action alone is not sufficient; ethics and compassion are also necessary. «سیئة مثلها» is the law, and «عفا و اصلح» is compassion and ethics.

7- Receiving Divine favors is conditional upon forgiveness and reconciliation, not retaliation. «فاجره على اللّه»

8- There is no reward in retaliation, but there is reward in forgiveness. «فمن عفا... فاجره على اللّه»

9- Encouragement for forgiveness and reconciliation is not meant to defend the oppressor. «انّه لا یحبّ الظالمین»

10- The oppressor is not beloved by Allah, whether he commits oppression initially or exceeds the bounds of justice in retaliation. «انّه لا یحبّ الظالمین»

11- Retaliation is for the one who has the power, but whoever lacks the ability has the right to seek assistance from others. «لمن انتصر بعد ظلمه»

12- Retribution before the crime is unjust. «بعد ظلمه...»

13- Retaliation against the oppressor is a legitimate human right, which is neither subject to blame in this world nor punishment in the Hereafter. «و لمن انتصر بعد ظلمه فاولئك ما علیهم من سبیل»

Ayahs

إِنَّمَا ٱلسَّبِيلُ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ يَظْلِمُونَ ٱلنَّاسَ وَيَبْغُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ لَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ (٤۲)
The ground for action is only against those who oppress the people and commit tyranny in the land in violation of justice. For such there will be a painful punishment. (42)
وَلَمَن صَبَرَ وَغَفَرَ إِنَّ ذَٰلِكَ لَمِنْ عَزْمِ ٱلْأُمُورِ (٤۳)
As for someone who endures patiently and forgives—that is indeed the steadiest of courses. (43)

Key Points

The Noble Quran states in Ayah 91 of Surah At-Tawbah: «ما على المحسنین من سبیل» (There is no cause [for blame] against the doers of good). There is no way of reproach against the doers of good. We also read in the previous Ayah that there is no hardship upon the oppressed. Now [this Ayah] states: Reproach is upon the oppressors. It seems that zulm (oppression) relates to the wicked, even if they are ordinary and poor individuals, but baghy (transgression/rebellion) relates to conspirators and colonialists.

Messages

1- Transgression against the rights of people (even non-Muslims, and in any form) is unlawful and deserves follow-up and accountability. «انّما السبیل على الّذین یظلمون الناس»

2- Oppression that must be exposed is that which has the color of sedition (fitnah), corruption (fasad), and a social dimension. «یظلمون الناس و یبغون فى الارض»

3- Although the duty of the people in this world is to seek help and expose [injustice], Allah will also hold the oppressors accountable. «لهم عذاب الیم»

4- Forgiving the oppressor is not a simple task; it requires firm will and resolve. «من عزم الامور»

5- Self-restraint and forgiveness (instead of revenge) are recommended by the Quran. «و لمن صبر و غفر ان ذلك من عزم الامور»

6- Those who are capable of forgiveness are those who are patient. (The word sabr [patience] precedes the word maghfirah [forgiveness]). «لمن صبر و غفر»

7- Patience and forgiveness are the foundation for outstanding human qualities. «و لمن صبر و غفر ان ذلك من عزم الامور»

8- Islam is a comprehensive religion that both recognizes the right of the oppressed and leaves the door open for pardon and forgiveness. «لهم عذاب الیم... و لمن صبر و غفر»

Ayah

وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِن وَلِىٍّ مِّنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ ۗ وَتَرَى ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ يَقُولُونَ هَلْ إِلَىٰ مَرَدٍّ مِّن سَبِيلٍ (٤٤)
Those whom Allah leads astray have no guardian apart from Him. You will see the wrongdoers, when they sight the punishment, saying, ‘Is there any way for a retreat?’ (44)

Key Points

Although in some verses of the Quran, the misguidance of the misguided is attributed to Allah, other verses interpret this attribution and state: «یضلّ اللّه مَن هو مسرف مرتاب»(61), Allah misguides those who are transgressors (in excess) and doubtful. «و ما یضلّ به الاّ الفاسقین»(62), Allah misguides the wicked. «و یضلّ اللّه الظالمین»(63), Allah misguides the wrongdoers. «یضلّ اللّه الكافرین»(64) Allah misguides the disbelievers. In other words, we do not have initial misguidance, but we do have punitive misguidance, whereby Allah leads individuals into the valley of misguidance as a punishment for their excess, disbelief, and wickedness.

61) Ghafir, 34. 62) Al-Baqarah, 26. 63) Ibrahim, 27. 64) Ghafir, 74.

Messages

1- Transgression against the rights of people leads to deprivation of guidance and falling into the whirlpool of misguidance, confusion, and regret. «یظلمون الناس و یبغون فى الارض... و من یضلل اللّه... هل الى مردّ من سبیل»

2- Let the wrongdoers and the deviated know that no individual or power will save them. «فما له من ولىّ»

3- Knowledge of the news of the Day of Judgment is only possible through revelation. «ترى الظالمین»

4- Contrary to the people of Paradise who receive whatever they desire, «لهم ما یشاؤن»(65) the people of Hell will not attain any wish. «هل الى مردّ من سبیل»65) Ash-Shura, 22.

Ayahs

وَتَرَىٰهُمْ يُعْرَضُونَ عَلَيْهَا خَـٰشِعِينَ مِنَ ٱلذُّلِّ يَنظُرُونَ مِن طَرْفٍ خَفِىٍّ ۗ وَقَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِنَّ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ ٱلَّذِينَ خَسِرُوٓا۟ أَنفُسَهُمْ وَأَهْلِيهِمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ فِى عَذَابٍ مُّقِيمٍ (٤٥)
You will see them being exposed to it, humbled by abasement, furtively looking askance. The faithful will say, ‘Indeed the losers are those who have ruined themselves and their families on the Day of Resurrection. Indeed, the wrongdoers will abide in lasting punishment. (45)
وَمَا كَانَ لَهُم مِّنْ أَوْلِيَآءَ يَنصُرُونَهُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَمَن يُضْلِلِ ٱللَّهُ فَمَا لَهُۥ مِن سَبِيلٍ (٤٦)
They have no awliya to help them besides Allah. There is no way out for those whom Allah leads astray.’ (46)

Key Points

In the two preceding verses, matters concerning the misguidance of the wrongdoers by Allah were mentioned. This verse contains greater threats. For instance, where He said (previously): "فما له من ولىّ" (there is no single individual who can help him), in this verse He says: "ما كان لهم من اولیاء" (there is no group that can help them).

Messages

1- The arrogant ones of today will be the humiliated ones of tomorrow. «تراهم... خاشعین من الذّل»

2- The terror of the Day of Judgment deprives the criminal of the power of sight. «ینظرون من طرف خفىّ»

3- It is possible for a person to be a loser, yet his children save him because they have attained perfection. The true loser is the one who is neither saved himself nor has seen any good from his children. «الّذین خسروا انفسهم و اهلیهم»

4- The real loser is the one who has both lost his capital (his existence and his life/age) «خسروا انفسهم و اهلیهم», and has no possibility of compensation or return. «یوم القیامة»

5- The people of faith will be honored and proud on the Day of Judgment, such that they will be able to reproach the oppressors. «قال الّذین آمنوا انّ الخاسرین...» (Perhaps the believers who say this to the oppressors are the very oppressed people who suffered injustice at their hands in the world.)

6- Describing the conditions of the Day of Judgment is for the purpose of awakening. «ألا انّ الظالمین» (The word "ألا" is for awareness and warning.)

Ayah

ٱسْتَجِيبُوا۟ لِرَبِّكُم مِّن قَبْلِ أَن يَأْتِىَ يَوْمٌ لَّا مَرَدَّ لَهُۥ مِنَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ مَا لَكُم مِّن مَّلْجَإٍ يَوْمَئِذٍ وَمَا لَكُم مِّن نَّكِيرٍ (٤۷)
Respond to your Lord before there comes a day for which there will be no revoking from Allah. On that day you will have no refuge, nor will you have [any chance for] denial [of your sins]. (47)

Key Points

Regarding what is meant by «یوم لا مردّ له من اللّه» (a Day from Allah which cannot be averted), there are two possibilities: A: The sinner will have no return to the world. B: That Day is definite and cannot be reconsidered.

Messages

1- What protects man from loss is following the prophets. (In the previous verses, there was talk of the losers; in this verse, He says the remedy is obedience to Allah. «استجیبوا لربّكم»)

2- Responding to God's commands is the cause of our nurturing, so we should listen to the words of the Educator. «استجیبوا لربّكم»

3- Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah) over man necessitates responding to His call. «استجیبوا لربّكم»

4- The remedy for an event must be applied before it occurs. (We must not lose opportunities and possibilities.) «من قبل ان یأتى یوم»

Ayah

فَإِنْ أَعْرَضُوا۟ فَمَآ أَرْسَلْنَـٰكَ عَلَيْهِمْ حَفِيظًا ۖ إِنْ عَلَيْكَ إِلَّا ٱلْبَلَـٰغُ ۗ وَإِنَّآ إِذَآ أَذَقْنَا ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ مِنَّا رَحْمَةً فَرِحَ بِهَا ۖ وَإِن تُصِبْهُمْ سَيِّئَةٌۢ بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ فَإِنَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ كَفُورٌ (٤۸)
But if they disregard [your warnings], [keep in mind that] We have not sent you as their keeper. Your duty is only to communicate. Indeed when We let man taste Our mercy, he boasts about it; but should an ill visit him because of what his hands have sent ahead, then man is very ungrateful. (48)

Key Points

The turning away of people is not always due to the performance of officials and leaders; even if an infallible Prophet preaches, a group will still turn away.

Messages

1- The leader of the community must anticipate the people's turning away from religion. «فان اعرضوا»

2- The responsibility of the Prophet is the conveyance of the message, not compelling people to accept it. «فما ارسلناك علیهم حفیظاً ان علیك الا البلاغ»

3- Blessings are from Allah, so they should not make us heedless of Him. «اذقنا... منّا رحمة»

4- Conveying the religion is obligatory upon the Prophet, even if some people do not accept it. «ان علیك الا البلاغ»

5- The pleasures of the world are nothing more than the measure of tasting. «اذقنا رحمة»

6- Whatever the mercy may be, it is from Allah. «منّا رحمة»

7- Man is a being of limited capacity. «اذقنا رحمة... فرح بها»

8- Mercies are from Allah, but calamities are the reflection of man's own deeds. «منّا رحمة... سیئة بما قدمت ایدیهم»

9- We must remember Allah even during hardships, otherwise we will be subject to blame and reproach. «تصیبهم سیّئة بما قدمت ایدیهم فان الانسان كفور»

Ayahs

لِّلَّهِ مُلْكُ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ يَخْلُقُ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ يَهَبُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ إِنَـٰثًا وَيَهَبُ لِمَن يَشَآءُ ٱلذُّكُورَ (٤۹)
To Allah belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He creates whatever He wishes; He gives females to whomever He wishes, and males to whomever He wishes, (49)
أَوْ يُزَوِّجُهُمْ ذُكْرَانًا وَإِنَـٰثًا ۖ وَيَجْعَلُ مَن يَشَآءُ عَقِيمًا ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِيمٌ قَدِيرٌ (٥٠)
or He combines them males and females, and makes sterile whomever He wishes. Indeed, He is all-knowing, all-powerful. (50)

Key Points

Since the Arabs of the Age of Ignorance (Jahiliyyah) considered the son superior to the daughter, Allah used the word "يَهَبُ" (He grants) in this Ayah regarding the creation of both (genders), so that He might make it understood that both the son and the daughter are divine gifts. And He brought the word "ذكور" (males/sons) with the letters alif and lam (the definite article 'Al-') to say: Those sons whom you cherish, they too are divine gifts. The meaning of "يُزَوِّجُهُمْ" (He pairs them) is that Allah sometimes grants both sons and daughters together.(66)

(66) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The disbelief and ingratitude of people have no effect on the sovereignty of Allah. "فَإِنَّ الْإِنْسَانَ كَفُورٌ لِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ..."

2- A child is a divine gift. "يَهَبُ" (He grants).

3- Let us entrust the gender of the child (son or daughter) to Him; He knows best. "يَهَبُ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ إِنَاثًا... عَلِيمٌ قَدِيرٌ"

4- An example of Allah's absolute sovereignty is that He grants whatever type of child He wills to whomever He wills. "لِلَّهِ مُلْكُ السَّمَاوَاتِ... يَهَبُ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ"

5- Dissatisfaction with having a daughter or a son is ingratitude (kufrān) for Allah's favor and His gift. "فَإِنَّ الْإِنْسَانَ كَفُورٌ... يَهَبُ لِمَنْ يَشَاءُ..."

6- Allah is continuously in the state of creation. "يَهَبُ... يَخْلُقُ... يَشَاءُ" (The imperfect tense [present/future verb] is a sign of continuity.)

7- Granting children or making a person barren is a manifestation of Allah's knowledge and power. "يَهَبُ... إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ قَدِيرٌ"

8- Being barren is not a sign of God's lack of power. "عَقِيمًا... قَدِيرٌ"

Ayah

۞ وَمَا كَانَ لِبَشَرٍ أَن يُكَلِّمَهُ ٱللَّهُ إِلَّا وَحْيًا أَوْ مِن وَرَآئِ حِجَابٍ أَوْ يُرْسِلَ رَسُولًا فَيُوحِىَ بِإِذْنِهِۦ مَا يَشَآءُ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ عَلِىٌّ حَكِيمٌ (٥۱)
It is not [possible] for any human that Allah should speak to him except through revelation or from behind a veil, or send a messenger who reveals by His permission whatever He wishes. Indeed He is all-exalted, all-wise. (51)

Key Points

The revelation (Wahy) descends upon the prophets in three ways: A: Direct inspiration (Alqa') into the heart of the Prophet. B: Hearing from behind a veil, such as the tree (referring to the incident with Moses). C: Through the angel carrying the revelation (Gabriel).

Messages

1- Revelation is from Allah, not the desire of man. «ما كان لبشر أن یكلّمه اللّه...»

2- Man can be addressed by Allah. «یكلّمه اللّه»

3- The content and measure of the revelation are in the hands of Allah. «ما یشاء»

4- Revelation originates from a high station and is based on wisdom. «انه علىّ حكیم»

Ayahs

وَكَذَٰلِكَ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَيْكَ رُوحًا مِّنْ أَمْرِنَا ۚ مَا كُنتَ تَدْرِى مَا ٱلْكِتَـٰبُ وَلَا ٱلْإِيمَـٰنُ وَلَـٰكِن جَعَلْنَـٰهُ نُورًا نَّهْدِى بِهِۦ مَن نَّشَآءُ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا ۚ وَإِنَّكَ لَتَهْدِىٓ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (٥۲)
Thus have We imbued you with a Spirit of Our command. You did not know what the Book is, nor what is faith; but We made it a light that We may guide by its means whomever We wish of Our servants. Indeed, you guide to a straight path, (52)
صِرَٰطِ ٱللَّهِ ٱلَّذِى لَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ أَلَآ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ تَصِيرُ ٱلْأُمُورُ (٥۳)
the path of Allah, to whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. Indeed all matters return to Allah! (53)

Key Points

This Surah began with the verse of revelation, «حم، عسق، كذلك یوحى الیك» (Hā Mīm. ‘Ayn Sīn Qāf. Thus does He reveal to you), and it concludes with the verse of revelation. «و كذلك اوحینا الیك» (And thus have We revealed to you).

Some have taken the meaning of ar-Rūḥ (the Spirit) in this verse to be the Trustworthy Spirit (Rūḥ al-Amīn), and others have taken it to be an angel superior to the rest of the angels who accompanies them, similar to «تنزّل الملائكة والروح فیها» (The angels and the Spirit descend therein) (67). A third group considers it to be the Qur’an, and in this view, this meaning seems better. The Spirit (Rūḥ) is the source of life and vitality, and the Qur’an is the symbol of man’s spiritual life. Just as the body without the spirit disintegrates and decays, a society without the Book and the Qur’an is exposed to disintegration. Just as the reality of the spirit is incomprehensible, the reality of the Qur’an is also incomprehensible, and just as the spirit does not become old, the Qur’an is also not subject to becoming old.

Imam Ali عليه السلام says in Nahj al-Balaghah: «لقد قرن اللّه به صلى اللّه علیه و آله من لدن كان فطیماً اعظم ملائكته یسلك به طریق المكارم و محاسن الاخلاق لیله و نهاره» (Indeed, Allah associated with him صلى اللّه علیه و آله, from the time he was weaned, the greatest of His angels, who guided him along the path of noble deeds and excellent morals night and day) (68).

Therefore, the meaning of the phrase «ولا الایمان» (nor the Faith) is not that the Prophet was abandoned, misguided, or faithless before the Prophetic mission (Bi’that), but rather that the Prophet was unaware of the details and contents of the Divine Law (A’īn).

For man’s spiritual journey, certain things are necessary, including: the path, «صراط مستقیم» (the straight path); the guide, «انّك لتهدى» (indeed you guide); the light, «و جعلناه نوراً» (and We made it a light); the map, «اوحینا الیك روحاً من امرنا» (We revealed to you a Spirit by Our command); and the goal and destination. «الى اللّه تصیر الامور» (To Allah return all affairs).

67) Al-Qadr, 4. 68) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 192.

Messages

1- Revelation to the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله is a continuation of revelation to previous prophets. «و كذلك اوحینا الیك»

2- The three types of revelation mentioned in the previous verse were for the Prophet of Islam. «و كذلك اوحینا الیك»

3- The Prophet of Islam was ummī (unlettered) and uneducated. «ما كنتَ تَدرى ما الكتاب»

4- Even the prophets cannot advance without Divine guidance. «ما كنتَ تَدرى...»

5- Purity and sincerity are signs of prophethood. Ordinary leaders are not willing to present their past in this manner to the people. «ما كنتَ تَدرى...»

6- Although guidance is in Allah’s hand «من نشاء» (whom We will), the foundation for guidance is the spirit of servitude and distance from arrogance. «عبادنا» (Our servants).

7- Whoever wishes to guide others must himself be established upon the path of truth. «انّك لمن المرسلین على صراط مستقیم» (Indeed, you are among the messengers, upon a straight path) (69), «انّك لتهدى الى صراط المستقیم» (Indeed, you guide to a straight path).

8- The Qur’an and the Messenger are means of guidance. «نوراً نهدى به... انك لتهدى»

9- The criterion is the current merit of individuals, even if their hands were empty in the past. «ما كنت تدرى... انك لتهدى»

10- Development (perfection) exists even for the prophets. «و ما كنت تدرى... انك لتهدى»

11- If Allah’s grace encompasses a person, the ignorant one of yesterday becomes the teacher of today. «ما كنت تدرى... انك لتهدى»

12- The Qur’an alone is not sufficient; even though the Qur’an is the light of guidance «نوراً نهدى به...» (a light by which We guide...), there is still a need for the Messenger. «انك لتهدى»

13- The straight path is the path of Allah. (Non-Divine schools of thought are incapable of determining the correct path for humanity.) «صراط مستقیم صراط اللّه الّذى»

14- We should follow the path of Him Who is the Sovereign of existence. «صراط اللّه الّذى له ما فى السموات...»

15- The people of faith should be joyful, and the people of obstinacy and disbelief should be warned that their ultimate affair rests with Allah. «الى اللّه تصیر الامور»

16- The movement of the universe is purposeful and evolutionary. «الى اللّه تصیر الامور»

69) Yā Sīn, 3-4.
